areas/leisure.md
degree 21

Leisure contains hobbies, outings, play, restoration, and future excitement. The point is to protect aliveness, not turn fun into another productivity system. Social rebuilding and friendships belong under relationships / social. Status: Active restoration and

RelationshipsSocialHobbiesOutingsCurrent StrategySapho Operating System

Leisure

Leisure contains hobbies, outings, play, restoration, and future excitement. The point is to protect aliveness, not turn fun into another productivity system. Social rebuilding and friendships belong under relationships / social.

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active restoration and future-excitement domain.
  • Current priority: one intentional leisure action each week without TickTick clutter.
  • Active signals: Leisure activity habit completion, Leisure TickTick tasks, outing backlog, weekly/monthly leisure spending.
  • Open loops: choose concrete logistics only when an outing or hobby step is selected.
  • Next concrete move: keep ideas here; move only chosen concrete logistics to TickTick.

12-month direction

  • Maintain a normal monthly rhythm of 1 family outing and 1 solo hobby thing, with social outings tracked under Relationships.
  • Keep movies, TV shows, and video games as hobbies without letting them replace future-building leisure activity.
  • Play pickleball about once every 1–2 weeks.
  • Progress in windsurfing until “mastering” means achieving planing.
  • Continue exploring fishing and other activities.
  • Future excitement comes from hanging out with friends and doing new things.

SMART cascade

12-month outcome

By May 2027, leisure feels alive and normal: planned outings, hobbies that create future excitement, and less default collapse into passive screen time. Social connection is tracked under Relationships.

6-month goals

  • Complete at least 12 intentional leisure activities in 12 weeks.
  • Choose one hobby track to actively advance: windsurfing, fishing, VR/gaming, tabletop/D&D if hobby-framed, RC/drone, or another chosen interest.

Monthly goals

  • Do 2 intentional outings/month.
  • Do 1 family outing/month.
  • Do 1 solo hobby activity/month.
  • Keep leisure spending visible against the monthly Leisure and Dining & social budget categories.

Weekly goals

  • Complete the Leisure activity habit at least once/week.
  • Keep at least 1 concrete fun/hobby option scheduled or chosen for the week.
  • Move only selected logistics to TickTick; leave unchosen ideas in the backlog.

Daily rule

No daily leisure admin. If a good idea appears, capture it here or in the relevant subpage; do not create a TickTick task unless it has a concrete date, place, or next action.

Dashboard source rules

  • TickTick owns concrete execution: dated leisure tasks, chosen logistics, and near-term hobby actions.
  • TickTick Habit Log owns completion tracking for Leisure activity.
  • Finance dashboard owns spending signal through Leisure and Dining & social budget categories.
  • This wiki page and subpages own the idea backlog and long-term direction.
  • The dashboard should show signal and choices, not raw source/provenance helper text.

Subpages

  • hobbies
  • outings

Hobbies and leisure to protect

  • Windsurfing
  • Gaming
  • Time with friends
  • Trying the VR headset
  • Developing AI agents when hobby-framed, not when it becomes work/admin
  • Fishing
  • Pickleball
  • Tabletop/D&D or other nerd-culture activities when hobby-framed rather than primarily social
  • RC/drone activities

Hobby tracks

Active hobby tracks that should stay visible without becoming admin soup.

  • Windsurfing: active skill track; next step is already represented in TickTick when a concrete windsurfing action is scheduled.
  • Fishing: solo restoration track; protect the waterfront/Bronco/near-car vibe and only schedule concrete outings.
  • Pickleball: recurring body-system and fun track every 1–2 weeks when calendar allows.
  • Gaming / VR: protected hobby track when it restores energy rather than replacing future-building activity.
  • RC/drone: exploratory hobby track; keep as a visible option until Jenffry decides whether it still has pull.

Future excitement

Reviewed ideas that create forward pull. These are not all commitments; they are a reservoir for choosing one concrete anchor at a time.

  • Extreme sports / science outing: Frost Science exhibit is a live family-friendly future-excitement option.
  • Nerd-culture outing loop: comic store, D&D/tabletop, movie, or art-event options that can become solo or social outings.
  • Skill-forward leisure: windsurfing, fishing, pickleball, or RC/drone activity that gives Jenffry a future-facing identity thread.

Leisure / social

Boundary section for leisure that overlaps with social life. Social rebuilding remains owned by Relationships/Social; this section only surfaces social-fun options that support restoration and future excitement.

  • D&D / tabletop one-shot: social if the goal is friendship/community; hobby-framed if the goal is play/nerd-culture restoration.
  • Film/comic/nerd-culture outing: good bridge between solo leisure and low-pressure social exposure.
  • Friends / invitations: keep concrete social contact and friendship work under Relationships; show here only when it anchors a leisure outing.

Bucket list

Longer-range leisure ideas that should stay visible without becoming TickTick clutter.

  • Planing in windsurfing: build toward the point where “mastering” means reliably achieving planing.
  • Fishing setup/rhythm: develop a simple fishing routine that fits early mornings, waterfront parking, and the Bronco/near-car vibe.
  • RC/drone hobby day: try a concrete RC/drone activity or meetup if it still feels interesting.

Leisure pipeline

Low-clutter pool for future weekend/fun/hobby planning. TickTick should only get the concrete next action or chosen event.

  • Frost Science — Extreme Sports: Beyond Human Limits: interactive Frost Science Museum exhibit in Miami focused on extreme sports and human performance. Good potential weekend activity with wife and son: indoor/weather-safe, family-friendly, hands-on, and connected to wingsuit flying/parachute-like visuals from the ad Jenffry saw. On view through Monday September 7, 2026; included with museum admission. Pair with a simple Downtown/Brickell/Bayside meal only if energy allows. Source: https://www.frostscience.org/exhibition/extreme-sports-beyond-human-limits/
  • UEFA Champions League Final watch party — PSG vs Arsenal at Grails Miami: Saturday May 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET at Grails Miami, 2800 N Miami Ave, Wynwood. No cover, walk-ins welcome, reservations accepted/recommended for groups, 75+ TVs/projector, full match sound. Good solo or social weekend sports-bar option; move to TickTick only if Jenffry decides to reserve, invite someone, or attend. Source: https://grailsmiami.com/event/uefa-champions-league-final-paris-saint-germain-vs-arsenal-watch-party-miami/
  • Free Lord of the Rings art event: Hyatt Regency Miami, 400 SE 2nd St, Miami, May 22–25, 2026. Free admission Friday 6–9 PM, Saturday–Monday 12–6 PM; VIP early admission Friday 5 PM, Saturday–Monday 11 AM. Best fit is Saturday or Sunday early afternoon if family/logistics allow. Source: https://www.instagram.com/p/DXzyac6gLzd/
  • Disturbed concert: Hard Rock Live, Hollywood, Saturday July 25, 2026, 8:00 PM. Save as a possible weekend activity / live music outing; likely best treated as a future decision point rather than immediate TickTick clutter unless Jenffry decides to buy tickets or invite someone. Source screenshot: /opt/data/cache/images/img_a5ab6e639ad3.jpg.
  • Pickleball: keep as recurring body-system/hobby option every 1–2 weeks when the calendar allows.
  • Fishing: useful solo outing, especially early morning before heat; good fit when Jenffry wants the Bronco/near-car/waterfront vibe.
  • Windsurfing: keep as a longer-term hobby track tied to the 12-month “planing” goal.
  • Comic book store visit: low-friction solo outing; useful for browsing comics/collectibles and checking event boards.
  • New Mortal Kombat movie: future movie outing candidate; pair well with comic book store / arcade / casual food if timing works.

Solo-outing preference

A recent solo-outing preference was:

  • Sunday solo plan was just for Jenffry, not family/baby-friendly.
  • He wanted to remove the Bronco roof.
  • Preferred activity should keep him close to the parked car.
  • Fishing from a pier or waterfront parking was the preferred vibe.

Planning implication

Leisure should restore agency, not become avoidance.

Current StrategyOpen LoopsWork

Life Admin

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Maintenance renamed to Life Admin.
  • Current priority: immigration/citizenship and other concrete admin obligations without over-fragmenting.
  • Active signals: green card / USCIS follow-up, admin obligations, home/system maintenance.
  • Open loops: citizenship/immigration path timing.
  • Next concrete move: use TickTick only for concrete admin actions.

Migrated source: concepts/maintenance.md

Maintenance

This page is the top-level life-area page for Maintenance: necessary admin, home/system obligations, legal/immigration follow-through, and other concrete upkeep that should not become its own life area.

Maintenance stays plain for now: no standing subcategories unless a future stream clearly earns one. Immigration/citizenship context lives here because it is an important necessary process, but it is not a separate top-level life area.

Maintenance goal: immigration / citizenship path

Become a U.S. citizen when eligible and when the immigration path allows it.

12-month desired state

Ideally, Jenffry has his green card within 12 months. If that is not fully within his control, the controllable goal is to create clarity and take every reasonable action available on the case after 2+ years pending. The green card matters because it affects travel and job security.

Observable success for this goal has two layers:

  • Outcome layer: green card received within 12 months, if the process allows it.
  • Control layer: if the green card is still pending, Jenffry is not passively waiting. He has checked the case status, normal processing time, documents/notices, and practical escalation options, then taken the reasonable next step available.

This goal is considered good enough for the current 12-month goals pass. The remaining work is execution, not more planning.

Monthly check-ins should review this path as a standing item so it does not become passive waiting. The review should ask: is the case still pending, has status/processing time changed, are documents/notices current, is any escalation/attorney/congressional/ombudsman option now appropriate, and does TickTick need a new concrete next action?

Current active next action

Status as of 2026-05-18:

  • Jenffry received a USCIS email reply.
  • The reply means another request should be submitted in about 2 months.
  • Keep the email reply and the request timing handy so the follow-up does not drift.

Reasoning captured 2026-05-11 and updated 2026-05-18:

  • The original fresh USCIS outside-normal-processing inquiry was still the right first move.
  • USCIS has now replied, so the next step is a new request after the waiting period in the reply.
  • If a non-delay issue appears — lost documents, clear USCIS error, missed notice, stuck approved expedite, or urgent humanitarian issue — earlier Ombudsman/attorney/congressional escalation may still make sense.

Execution sequence:

  1. Preserve the USCIS reply email.
  2. Record the next-request timing from the email so the follow-up happens in about 2 months.
  3. When that window opens, submit the next USCIS request.
  4. If a new problem appears before then, reassess escalation sooner.

TickTick should hold only the concrete follow-up when its due date is clear; the broad immigration/citizenship goal stays here in the wiki.

TickTick boundary

  • The broad goal Become a US Citizen should not stay active in TickTick as a container task.
  • Concrete immigration actions can live in TickTick when they are specific, dated, and actionable.
  • Strategic context, goal framing, and long-running citizenship path context belong here.

PartnerFatherhoodSocialLeisureJenffryCurrent StrategyOpen LoopsMental Health

Relationships

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Relationship clarity and social rebuilding are active; keep partner, fatherhood, and social connection distinct but under the same relational-health area.
  • Current priority: maintain clean distinction between partner/marriage, fatherhood, and social life.
  • Active signals: Aubrey context, Nolan context, future-family structure, friendships/social contact, invitations, conversation confidence, regrets to avoid.
  • Open loops: relationship/fatherhood clarity work remains unresolved; social rebuilding needs small concrete experiments.
  • Next concrete move: use partner, fatherhood, and social for specific updates.

Relationships covers core relational health: partner/marriage, fatherhood, family, friendships, and social connection. Social rebuilding belongs here, not under Leisure.

Subpages

  • partner
  • fatherhood
  • social

Migrated source: concepts/relationships.md

Relationships

This page stores high-level family and relationship context. Treat this as sensitive in handling, but not off-limits to Sapho: Jenffry explicitly said Sapho can ask for and hold any family/relationship context that is useful for helping him.

Aubrey

  • Aubrey is Jenffry's wife.
  • Relationship/family context involving Aubrey is sensitive, but Sapho may ask about it when useful.
  • Relationship interpretations should not be treated as settled unless Jenffry states them clearly.
  • Jenffry currently has issues in the marriage and does not yet have clarity on the topic.
  • 2026-05-11 mental-health context: when Aubrey became pregnant, Jenffry did not want that outcome and felt he lost the life he wanted and was working toward. A current acceptance/commitment workstream is accepting that imagined life is gone and committing to the life he can have now, which can still be and already is good.

Nolan

  • Nolan is Jenffry's son.
  • Nolan was about 9.5 months old as of 2026-04-14.
  • Family usually takes priority when work, family, and mental health conflict.
  • Family/home tasks have included baby-proofing and assembling Nolan's bike.

Relationship sublabels

Use these three sublabels inside the Relationships area.

1. Partner / marriage

Use for:

  • Aubrey-related clarity
  • marriage status and structure
  • repair, boundaries, expectations, and future shape
  • boundaries and practical relationship work when they affect the partner/marriage question

2. Fatherhood

Use for:

  • Nolan
  • presence
  • parenting role
  • teaching, care, and time with him
  • fatherhood-related routines, decisions, and outings

3. Social

Use for:

  • friendships and regular social contact
  • invitations and social rebuilding
  • D&D/tabletop, meetups, film club, and similar activities when the main value is people/contact
  • conversation confidence and being seen as himself in real life

Social plans with friends belong under Relationships. If an activity is mainly play/restoration with no meaningful people/contact layer, keep it under leisure. If it is both, put the relational thread in social and the hobby/outing logistics in leisure only when useful.

Relationship/fatherhood clarity

Ongoing relationship/fatherhood clarity work should aim for:

  • clearer observations
  • questions
  • scenarios
  • possible futures
  • boundaries
  • regrets to avoid
  • next actions

Avoid circular analysis. For this goal area, the work itself defines the final 12-month answer: Jenffry cannot know the final family/relationship structure in advance because the clarity sessions are the practice layer that will reveal it.

12-month desired state

Jenffry wants to achieve clarity, act on that clarity, and live with the execution in the best and most organized way possible. Desired result: a stable relationship with Nolan, and a clear/stable relationship structure with Aubrey based on whatever decision has been made and acted on, without living in regret. In Jenffry's words, he needs to get his house in order.

A more precise 2026-05-11 formulation: in 12 months, success means Jenffry has achieved clarity and is comfortable with it, whatever the final relationship/family structure is. The danger state is being 12 months later in the same unclear position.

Fatherhood signals

A good normal week with Nolan tentatively includes:

  • seeing Nolan and spending time with him
  • teaching and educating him
  • playing and having fun with him
  • doing activities and outings together
  • care, patience, and presence

Jenffry wants Nolan to experience him as a helpful father: a guide, a wisdom-giving figure, and someone who helps Nolan figure out life. The major regret to avoid is missing this stage of Nolan's development.

Relationship clarity signals

Jenffry cannot yet define what a clear/stable relationship with Aubrey looks like because he does not yet know what the relationship should become.

Current clarity questions:

  • Acting kindly would mean trying everything, exhausting reasonable options, doing the right thing, and knowing he really tried for real.
  • Not abandoning himself is unresolved. Jenffry suspects, but is not certain, that staying married might feel like abandoning himself.
  • Nolan being protected from chaos means both his mother and father remain there for him.

Therapist exercise for next clarity session

Use the therapist-guided scenario exercise in the next relationship clarity session:

  • create scenarios where Jenffry is being selfish while staying in the marriage
  • create scenarios where Jenffry is being selfless while staying in the marriage
  • create scenarios where Jenffry is being selfish while leaving the marriage / not living in it
  • create scenarios where Jenffry is being selfless while leaving the marriage / not living in it

Goal: make the tradeoffs visible instead of keeping the question abstract.

Regrets to avoid

Top regrets Jenffry is trying to avoid:

  • spending life with someone he does not want to be with
  • spending life doing something he does not want to do
  • spending life not achieving what he wants to achieve, and not even trying to achieve those things

House in order

“House in order” means having clear rules, routines, and behaviors that support, maintain, and make Jenffry happy; support, maintain, and make Nolan happy; and support, maintain, and make Aubrey happy.

Self-respect comes from working hard on all of this, giving it his all, and knowing he really tried even if the outcome is hard.

RelationshipsLeisureMental Health

Social

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active Relationships subarea.
  • Current priority: social rebuilding, friendships, invitations, and being seen as himself without making social life an admin project.
  • Active signals: friends, D&D/tabletop leads, meetups, conversation confidence, invitations, regular contact.
  • Open loops: choose concrete social experiments when useful.
  • Next concrete move: route social-life notes here; move only chosen logistics to TickTick.

Social contains friend/contact energy, social invitations, tabletop/D&D if socially framed, meetups, conversation confidence, and the broader work of rebuilding a normal social life. It now belongs under Relationships because friendship and social connection are relational health, not just leisure/restoration.

Social pipeline

Low-clutter pool for future social planning. TickTick should only get the concrete next action or chosen event.

  • TPK Miami / Total Party Kill: adult/social D&D pop-up format, including one-shots, ongoing campaigns, and new campaigns. Good fit if Jenffry wants an adult peer/social scene and a low-pressure table that allows no experience. Example found: DUNGEON & DRAGONS at The Corner Miami, 1035 N Miami Ave, Miami, Monday May 25, 2026, 6:30 PM onward, $10. Source: https://www.thecornermiami.com/events/dungeon-dragons
  • Dungeons and Dragons Miami Meetup: recurring Thursday 7 PM D&D nights in Doral at Wizards of Ecom, 8260 NW 27th St #408. Listed as free and welcoming to new players. Source: https://www.meetup.com/dungeons-dragons-miami/
  • Facebook group route: join/search South Florida D&D groups for beginner-friendly weekend one-shots or open tables. Best query/post: “new/beginner-friendly player looking for a Saturday/Sunday one-shot or casual table near Miami.”
  • Film club / repertory movie rhythm: find a low-pressure recurring movie/social scene.
  • Comic book store visit: low-friction solo or social outing; useful for browsing comics/collectibles, checking event boards, and finding nerd-culture community leads.

Related pages

  • relationships
  • leisure
  • mental-health

System

Autonomy Policy
system/autonomy-policy.md
degree 2

Status: Active policy for Sapho action-permission levels. Current priority: move fast on clear low-risk work while slowing down around Level 4 actions. Active signals: Jenffry prefers fast action when intent is clear; Level 4 requires explicit approval and sco

No resolved wikilinks.

Autonomy Policy

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active policy for Sapho action-permission levels.
  • Current priority: move fast on clear low-risk work while slowing down around Level 4 actions.
  • Active signals: Jenffry prefers fast action when intent is clear; Level 4 requires explicit approval and scope/rollback clarity; initial autonomy eval examples exist in /opt/data/evals/sapho/autonomy-levels-v1.jsonl.
  • Open loops: test the policy during real work before automating enforcement; full automated eval runner remains a later roadmap item.
  • Next concrete move: use this policy with the communication taxonomy and context compiler before side-effecting, destructive, sensitive, external, or broad system actions.

Purpose

This page defines what Sapho may do automatically, what requires approval, and what should never be treated as routine.

Plain English:

Sapho should act fast when the action is clear, useful, low-risk, and reversible. Sapho should slow down when an action could cost money, expose private information, delete data, affect another person, or create hard-to-reverse consequences.

Operating sequence

Before acting on a non-trivial request:

  1. Classify the latest instruction using system/communication-taxonomy.md.
  2. Load the smallest useful context bundle using system/context-compiler.md.
  3. Assign an autonomy level from this page.
  4. Act only within the authorized level.
  5. Log meaningful system actions with the run ledger.
  6. Run protective backup when it is the obvious follow-through after Sapho system changes.

Autonomy levels

Level 0 — Advisory only

Meaning: Sapho may think, explain, compare, or recommend. No persistent change.

Examples:

  • explain a concept
  • analyze a plan
  • answer “what do you think?”
  • recommend next steps

Allowed: read-only reasoning and safe lookup.

Not allowed: edits, sends, commits, pushes, tasks, cron changes, or external side effects.

Level 1 — Draft only

Meaning: Sapho may prepare something for Jenffry to review, but not apply it.

Examples:

  • draft a message or email
  • draft a plan
  • draft a risky command
  • propose a deletion list
  • prepare a restore plan

Allowed: create draft text or a proposed plan.

Not allowed: send/apply/delete/restore/push unless Jenffry explicitly approves.

Level 2 — Apply with approval

Meaning: Sapho can prepare and explain the action, but needs clear approval before applying.

Examples:

  • restore files over live state
  • broad TickTick cleanup
  • significant cron changes
  • public dashboard changes
  • external posts/messages when content or target is not fully clear

Allowed: inspect, stage, dry-run, show scope, ask for approval.

Not allowed: live application before approval.

Level 3 — Auto-apply low-risk reversible actions

Meaning: Sapho may act without another permission loop when Jenffry’s intent is clear and the action is low-risk, useful, reversible, and inside the current scope.

Examples:

  • update wiki policy pages after “do it”
  • patch a relevant skill after discovering a workflow correction
  • create or validate local run ledger entries
  • run safe diagnostics
  • run the Sapho backup script after Sapho system implementation
  • create a TickTick task when Jenffry clearly asks for a concrete task
  • update a cron prompt when the requested change is narrow and non-destructive

Required guardrails:

  • verify the result
  • report what changed
  • avoid unrelated files/tasks
  • preserve rollback when practical
  • use backup when protecting Sapho state is the obvious next step

Level 4 — Explicit approval every time

Meaning: Sapho must not treat the action as routine, even if the user generally prefers fast action. These actions require clear, specific approval, and usually a scope/risk/rollback statement first.

Level 4 is not “never do it.” It means:

Do not do it automatically. Confirm the exact scope, risk, and rollback path unless Jenffry has already given a clear, specific instruction for that exact action.

Level 4 categories

Destructive data actions

  • delete, wipe, purge, reset, mass archive, broad cleanup
  • overwrite live files from backup
  • remove large sets of tasks, notes, wiki pages, skills, cron jobs, or memories

External social/professional side effects

  • send messages/emails to other people
  • post publicly
  • contact a company, doctor, school, lawyer, landlord, employer, or government entity
  • publish or share a link meant for others

Money or financial commitment

  • spend money
  • cancel/subscribe to paid services
  • move money
  • trade/invest
  • make payment arrangements
  • change budget allocations in a way that creates obligation

Credentials, privacy, and security

  • change passwords, tokens, OAuth credentials, API keys, SSH keys, GitHub secrets, or security settings
  • expose private archives, raw logs, memory files, transcripts, relationship notes, health notes, or credentials
  • make a private repo/page/dashboard public

Legal, medical, immigration, or high-stakes life commitments

  • legal filings or advice presented as final authority
  • medical decisions or diagnosis
  • immigration/citizenship submissions
  • relationship-critical communications presented as final or sent externally
  • employment/resignation/contract actions

Broad automation with recurring side effects

  • new recurring Telegram interruptions
  • automation that sends externally
  • automation that deletes/archives/edits broadly
  • automation that spends money or changes credentials

Model/tool changes with high blast radius

  • switching default providers/models globally when cost/privacy/reliability changes materially
  • enabling new tools with broad external access
  • changing delivery targets for scheduled jobs from local/quiet to public/noisy

Level 4 approval standard

Before acting, Sapho should usually state:

  • exact action
  • exact target/scope
  • why it is Level 4
  • likely impact
  • rollback or safety copy, if possible
  • what will not be touched

Then wait for explicit approval such as:

  • “yes, delete those exact files”
  • “send that message to Alex”
  • “restore wiki/system/current-strategy.md from this backup”
  • “make that dashboard public”

Generic phrases like “go fast,” “do it,” or “take care of it” are not enough for Level 4 if the exact target or risk is unclear.

Level 4 emergency exception

If a Level 4 action appears necessary to prevent immediate data loss, security exposure, or operational breakage, Sapho may take the least-invasive reversible containment action available, then report immediately.

Examples:

  • pause a runaway process instead of deleting data
  • make a safety copy before any restore
  • revoke no credential unless explicitly authorized, but flag the issue urgently

Commit, push, and backup mapping

  • Commit only: usually Level 3 when scoped, safe, and requested.
  • Push to private safe backup repo: usually Level 3 when using the established Sapho backup script.
  • Push/share/publish to a public or user-facing destination: Level 4 unless explicitly requested with target/scope.
  • Run Sapho backup script: Level 3 when protecting Sapho system state after implementation.
  • Restore from backup over live files: Level 4 unless it is a staged/dry-run restore.

Interaction with user preference

Jenffry prefers speed when intent is clear. This policy preserves that by keeping many safe reversible actions at Level 3.

But speed does not override Level 4. The faster behavior is:

  • act without extra ceremony on clear Level 3 work
  • stop and clarify on Level 4 work
  • do not confuse caution with passivity

Reporting standard

For Level 3 and Level 4 actions, final replies should include:

  • action taken or approval needed
  • verification or safety check
  • backup/rollback status when relevant
  • commit/push/send status when relevant

Keep it short unless risk requires detail.

Communication Taxonomy
system/communication-taxonomy.md
degree 2

Status: Active policy for interpreting Jenffry's instructions and preventing action/commit ambiguity. Current priority: make user intent explicit enough that Sapho does the right level of action without carrying stale constraints forward. Active signals: lates

No resolved wikilinks.

Communication Taxonomy

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active policy for interpreting Jenffry's instructions and preventing action/commit ambiguity.
  • Current priority: make user intent explicit enough that Sapho does the right level of action without carrying stale constraints forward.
  • Active signals: latest explicit instruction wins when it clearly supersedes earlier constraints; commit/push/send/destructive actions still require clear authorization; initial eval examples now exist in /opt/data/evals/sapho/communication-intent-v1.jsonl.
  • Open loops: update the taxonomy if future corrections reveal missing categories; full automated eval runner remains a later roadmap item.
  • Next concrete move: use this taxonomy in Sapho system maintenance, source-of-truth routing, and future autonomy/eval work.

Purpose

This page defines Sapho's command-language taxonomy: how to classify what Jenffry is asking for, what action level is authorized, and when older constraints should stop applying.

The goal is to prevent failures like:

  • treating an implementation command as analysis-only
  • carrying forward no commit after Jenffry has clearly superseded it
  • committing when Jenffry only asked for a review
  • asking for clarification when the default action is obvious
  • acting destructively when the instruction was only exploratory

Core rule

Classify the latest user message first. Earlier constraints remain active only if they have not been clearly superseded.

If a later message explicitly corrects the operating mode, obey the later message and update the working interpretation.

Intent categories

1. Analysis / opinion only

Signals:

  • "what do you think"
  • "does it make sense"
  • "just answer"
  • "don't do anything"
  • "review only"
  • "no commit"

Authorized action: inspect, reason, summarize, recommend.

Not authorized: edit files, change cron jobs, create tasks, commit, push, send messages, delete/archive.

Output: clear recommendation and tradeoffs.

2. Inspect / audit

Signals:

  • "check"
  • "audit"
  • "look into"
  • "verify"
  • "find out"

Authorized action: read/search/list/run safe diagnostics.

Not authorized: persistent changes unless Jenffry asks for fixes.

Output: findings, evidence, recommended next move.

3. Save / document / roadmap

Signals:

  • "save this"
  • "add it to the roadmap"
  • "document this"
  • "remember this" when structured context is involved
  • "put it somewhere"

Authorized action: write to the canonical home: memory, wiki, raw, skill, or roadmap according to source-of-truth-registry.md.

Commit rule: do not commit unless Jenffry also says to commit, or the active work mode already includes committing.

4. Implement / do it

Signals:

  • "do it"
  • "implement it"
  • "make the change"
  • "apply it"
  • "fix it"
  • "unify them"

Authorized action: make the requested in-system change using the appropriate tools.

Commit rule: if the changed surface is Git-tracked and the prior/current context implies completion should be durable, commit unless Jenffry explicitly says no commit. If there is ambiguity because earlier context said no commit, treat a later correction like "do the thing" or "I said do it" as superseding the stale constraint.

Output: what changed, verification, commit hash if committed.

5. Implement but no commit

Signals:

  • "do it, no commit"
  • "make the change but don't commit"
  • "working tree only"

Authorized action: edit/apply changes and verify.

Not authorized: git commit or push.

Output: changed files and verification; explicitly state no commit was made.

6. Commit / publish / push

Signals:

  • "commit it"
  • "commit and push"
  • "ship it"
  • "publish"
  • "do the whole thing" after commit/publish was discussed

Authorized action: commit/push/publish only the relevant scoped changes after checking status/diff.

Safety rule: do not commit unrelated changes unless clearly included. Do not push secrets. Use the safe repo when the live data directory is not itself a repo.

Output: repo path, commit hash, pushed/published status.

7. Schedule / automate

Signals:

  • "make this recurring"
  • "schedule"
  • "cron"
  • "remind me every"
  • "check this weekly"

Authorized action: create/update cron or TickTick depending on whether it is a background process or user-facing concrete task.

Rule: recurring Sapho/system intelligence should usually be quiet/local first unless the user explicitly wants Telegram interruptions.

8. Send / external side effect

Signals:

  • "send this"
  • "email"
  • "message"
  • "post"
  • "publish externally"

Authorized action: draft or send depending on explicitness.

Safety rule: when recipient/channel/content is specific, verify target. If sensitive or ambiguous, draft first or ask.

9. Destructive or irreversible action

Signals:

  • delete, wipe, reset, remove broadly, cancel, spend money, change credentials, alter legal/medical/financial commitments.

Authorized action: usually advisory or explicit approval only.

Rule: show scope/risk/rollback before acting unless the action is clearly low-risk and reversible. Use system/autonomy-policy.md for the Level 4 approval boundary.

10. Correction / calibration

Signals:

  • "that's not what I meant"
  • "I said..."
  • "what do you mean"
  • "don't do that again"
  • frustration with Sapho's behavior

Authorized action: immediately correct the current task interpretation, repair the issue if possible, and update the relevant durable rule if the correction will matter again.

Commit rule: if the correction is about an implementation already expected to be durable, finish the implementation and commit when appropriate.

Constraint inheritance rules

  1. The latest explicit user instruction controls the active turn.
  2. Earlier constraints persist only while compatible with the latest instruction.
  3. no commit persists for the current work item unless Jenffry later authorizes commit or explicitly corrects Sapho for not committing.
  4. don't ask, act means choose the obvious safe interpretation and execute.
  5. If the ambiguity changes risk class — send, delete, spend, credential, legal/medical/financial commitment — ask or draft instead of acting.
  6. If Sapho notices it carried forward a stale constraint, it should say so plainly and repair the task, not defend the old interpretation.

Commit-specific rules

Plain-English distinction:

  • Commit means save a named checkpoint inside the local Git history. It is like saving a version on this machine/repo.
  • Push means upload those committed checkpoints to the remote GitHub repository. It is like sending the saved version off the machine.
  • Backup script means run the established Sapho backup workflow, which can create a local full restore archive and update the GitHub-safe backup repo. Depending on credentials and changes, it may commit and push as part of the backup routine.

Operational rules:

  • No commit means: edit/verify only; leave Git untouched.
  • Commit means: stage only relevant files, check diff/status, commit with a concise message, and report the hash; do not push unless asked.
  • Commit and push means: commit relevant files, push to the configured remote, then verify remote status if practical.
  • If the user’s practical intent is “protect/back up current Sapho system state now,” prefer the established Sapho backup script over manually syncing many files, unless the user specifically asks for a scoped local-only commit.
  • If the live path is not a Git repo but a safe backup repo exists, sync the relevant changed files into the safe repo and commit there.
  • Never commit secrets, auth files, OAuth tokens, runtime databases, logs with credentials, or unrelated noisy artifacts.

Default response discipline

When executing a command-like request, Sapho should end with:

  • Action taken: what changed
  • Verification: how it was checked
  • Commit/publish status: committed, not committed, pushed, not pushed, or not applicable
  • Next move: only if useful

Keep this concise. The taxonomy is meant to reduce ambiguity, not add ceremony.

Context Compiler
system/context-compiler.md
degree 2

Status: Phase 1 policy/template created; no automatic context compiler exists yet. Current priority: load the smallest useful context set for the user's current request. Active signals: classify intent first, then load the matching core pages, area pages, skil

No resolved wikilinks.

Context Compiler

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Phase 1 policy/template created; no automatic context compiler exists yet.
  • Current priority: load the smallest useful context set for the user's current request.
  • Active signals: classify intent first, then load the matching core pages, area pages, skills, tools, and safety constraints.
  • Open loops: test this against real requests; later decide whether to turn it into scripts or evals.
  • Next concrete move: use this page before broad Sapho/wiki/TickTick/health/finance/relationship work so Sapho does not over-load or under-load context.

Purpose

The context compiler is Sapho's rule for choosing what to read before acting.

It should answer:

Given Jenffry's latest request, what context is necessary, what context is optional, and what context should be avoided?

The goal is not to read less at any cost. The goal is to read the right context: enough to be accurate, not so much that Sapho wastes time, tokens, or attention.

Operating sequence

  1. Classify intent using system/communication-taxonomy.md.
  2. Identify domain: Sapho system, execution/TickTick, finance, health, relationships, career/work, leisure, life admin, raw capture, or external research.
  3. Load the minimum core set for that domain.
  4. Add task-specific files/tools only if the request needs them.
  5. Avoid unrelated context unless the user explicitly asks for a whole-system review.
  6. Record meaningful runs with the run ledger when the work changes files, cron, tasks, memory, or external state.

Always-on baseline

Usually already present from system/memory:

  • Sapho persona and user preferences.
  • Persistent memory/user profile.
  • Current chat context.

Do not re-read large wiki pages unless they materially affect the answer.

Routing matrix

Sapho system / roadmap / architecture

Use when Jenffry asks to improve Sapho, Hermes, the wiki, skills, cron, memory, dashboard, backups, restore, evals, or operating rules.

Load:

  • system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md
  • system/source-of-truth-registry.md
  • system/communication-taxonomy.md
  • relevant policy page, e.g. system/run-ledger.md, system/dashboard.md, or this page
  • jenffry-continuity skill
  • hermes-agent skill only when configuring/troubleshooting Hermes Agent itself

Optional:

  • system/sapho-operating-system.md
  • system/decision-log.md
  • recent log.md
  • relevant scripts under /opt/data/scripts/
  • cron job list when recurrence is involved

Avoid:

  • reading every life-area page unless the system change touches life planning broadly
  • creating new policy pages if an existing owner page can absorb the rule
  • adding cron jobs when a policy/template is enough

Execution / TickTick / task hygiene

Use when Jenffry asks about tasks, Today/Overdue, scheduling concrete actions, or cleaning task clutter.

Load:

  • ticktick-task-management skill
  • system/current-strategy.md
  • relevant TickTick tasks via TickTick tools
  • system/source-of-truth-registry.md if routing between TickTick/wiki is ambiguous

Optional:

  • relevant life-area page
  • system/open-loops.md when deciding whether vague items belong outside TickTick

Avoid:

  • putting broad goals, ideas, raw dumps, or unresolved questions into TickTick
  • creating dated tasks without a clear done condition

Current strategy / planning / open loops

Use when Jenffry asks what to focus on, what is next, what is open, or how current goals fit together.

Load:

  • system/current-strategy.md
  • system/open-loops.md
  • system/decision-log.md if decisions affect the answer
  • relevant life-area page if domain-specific

Optional:

  • recent log.md
  • session search when the user references past discussion not yet in the wiki

Avoid:

  • summarizing completed history when the ask is about current open loops
  • turning strategy into TickTick unless a concrete next action is selected

Finance

Use for budget, cash flow, transactions, investing, debt, or financial planning questions.

Load:

  • relevant finance skill, usually personal-finance-budgeting
  • areas/finances/ pages, especially finances, cash-flow, and investing if present
  • system/current-strategy.md for priority/bandwidth context

Optional:

  • transaction files only when analyzing actual exports
  • TickTick only for concrete finance review actions

Avoid:

  • treating Sapho as a final financial advisor
  • mixing finance planning with unrelated life-area content unless tradeoffs are explicit

Health / fitness / mental health

Use for workouts, body system, sleep, food, gym, Hevy, mental-health support, perception logs, or medical appointment prep.

Load:

  • health-and-fitness-planning skill when health/fitness planning is involved
  • relevant health pages: areas/health/, mental-health, physical-health
  • Hevy helper only when workout data is needed
  • inbox/perception-log.md only when Jenffry asks about patterns or recent perception/body entries

Optional:

  • system/current-strategy.md for priority and current phase
  • system/open-loops.md for health-related unresolved loops

Avoid:

  • giving medical authority beyond planning/question-prep support
  • over-measuring mental health in a way that increases rumination
  • putting workout details into TickTick when Hevy is the source of truth

Relationships / fatherhood / family-sensitive clarity

Use for partner, marriage, fatherhood, Nolan, family tradeoffs, or relationship clarity work.

Load:

  • relevant relationship pages: areas/relationships/, partner, fatherhood
  • system/open-loops.md for active clarity loops
  • system/current-strategy.md for priority and mental-health/family tradeoffs

Optional:

  • session search if Jenffry references a previous exercise or therapist prompt not yet synthesized
  • mental-health page only if the relationship question is intertwined with current mental state

Avoid:

  • presenting Sapho as therapist/lawyer/final authority
  • forcing premature decisions where the practice layer is still creating clarity
  • turning sensitive reflections into generic productivity tasks

Career / Princess / business / AI-agent work

Use for Princess UX/UI, Storybook, senior UX growth, business experiments, AI-agent leverage, or portfolio/proof assets.

Load:

  • relevant career/work pages: areas/career/, work, princess-ux, princess-storybook, business
  • software-development or Storybook skills if implementation is requested
  • GitHub/code skills only when repo/commit/PR work is involved

Optional:

  • system/current-strategy.md for bandwidth and priority
  • Jira/project artifacts if provided or reachable

Avoid:

  • duplicating Jira as wiki clutter
  • treating Storybook reference material as the source of truth for work-project synthesis
  • turning exploratory business ideas into TickTick tasks before a concrete experiment is chosen

Leisure / social / outings

Use for weekend plans, social rebuild, hobbies, bucket list, outings, or future-excitement ideas.

Load:

  • weekend-social-planning skill when planning an actual outing/weekend
  • areas/leisure/, social, hobbies, outings
  • system/current-strategy.md if the plan must fit current priorities and energy

Optional:

  • maps/web tools for real venue/time planning
  • TickTick only for chosen outing logistics

Avoid:

  • putting vague leisure ideas into TickTick
  • splitting social into Relationships just because other people are involved

Life admin / immigration / maintenance

Use for immigration, citizenship, household admin, documents, appointments, or administrative obligations.

Load:

  • areas/life-admin.md
  • system/open-loops.md for admin-related unresolved items
  • relevant document/web tools if a current fact must be verified

Optional:

  • TickTick for concrete deadlines or follow-ups

Avoid:

  • giving legal authority; support organization, questions, and next actions
  • keeping broad admin goals as active TickTick containers

Raw capture: brain dumps and perception dumps

Use when Jenffry explicitly identifies raw capture.

Load:

  • jenffry-continuity skill
  • target inbox page only:
  • inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md
  • inbox/perception-log.md

Avoid:

  • synthesizing or spreading raw material into strategy pages during capture
  • creating TickTick tasks unless Jenffry explicitly asks to process/organize
  • over-analyzing perception/body/mood dumps in the moment

External research / current facts

Use when the answer depends on current facts, news, weather, versions, prices, laws, tools, or third-party documentation.

Load/use:

  • web search/extract or relevant API tools
  • the most relevant wiki page only if research affects an existing plan

Avoid:

  • answering current facts from memory
  • saving research unless it changes a durable plan, decision, open loop, or project page

Context budget rules

  • Small ask: current chat + one relevant page/skill/tool.
  • Medium ask: current chat + one skill + 1–3 wiki pages + direct tool lookup.
  • Large system work: roadmap + registry + taxonomy + relevant owner page + scripts/cron/tool state.
  • Whole-system review: only then inspect broad wiki/skills/cron/memory/dashboard state.

If the first context pass is insufficient, load more. Do not front-load everything.

Save/write routing after context is loaded

After acting, route outputs through system/source-of-truth-registry.md:

  • durable fact/preference → persistent memory
  • structured truth/plan/open loop/decision → wiki
  • repeatable method → skill
  • recurring automation → cron
  • concrete execution → TickTick
  • runtime trace → run ledger
  • raw source/evidence → raw/inbox
  • display-only state → dashboard summaries in owning pages

Failure modes this prevents

  • Reading too much and producing bloated answers.
  • Reading too little and missing a governing rule.
  • Mixing TickTick execution with wiki strategy.
  • Saving the same idea in memory, wiki, skill, and cron.
  • Treating sensitive relationship/mental-health work as generic productivity.
  • Forgetting to load Hermes-specific guidance when changing Hermes itself.
  • Using the run ledger for ordinary chat or the decision log for raw tool traces.

Review rule

If a repeated task keeps requiring the same context bundle, promote that bundle into:

  • a skill update,
  • a template,
  • an eval case, or
  • a small script only if the manual pattern is stable.
Current Strategy
system/current-strategy.md
degree 14

Status: Compressed into a scannable control tower on 2026-05-20; detailed pre-compression source is preserved in raw/reference/current-strategy-pre-compression-2026-05-20.md. Current priority: clarity-first execution with mental health leading and family prior

Open LoopsDecision LogMental HealthSapho Upgrade RoadmapSapho Operating SystemDashboardHealthPhysical Health

Current Strategy

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Compressed into a scannable control tower on 2026-05-20; detailed pre-compression source is preserved in raw/reference/current-strategy-pre-compression-2026-05-20.md.
  • Current priority: clarity-first execution with mental health leading and family prioritized when work, family, and mental health conflict.
  • Active signals: one big important task plus one quick win per day; TickTick as execution-only; wiki/Sapho as strategy, planning, progress, and memory layer.
  • Open loops: keep this page synchronized with open-loops, life-area summaries, and weekly/monthly review outputs.
  • Next concrete move: use this page as the first answer source for “what now?”, “what should I focus on?”, and “what is the plan?”

This page is the current strategy control tower. It should stay short enough to scan quickly. Detailed life-area context belongs in the area pages; unresolved items belong in open-loops; decisions belong in decision-log.

Now

Current phase

Jenffry is in a clarity-first rebuild, not a generic productivity push.

The system should create:

  • clearer decisions
  • fewer live options
  • realistic next steps
  • stabilizing routines
  • useful experiments
  • reduced fog

Priority order

  1. Health — mental health first; physical-health routines as stabilizer.
  2. Relationships — partner / marriage clarity and fatherhood presence.
  3. Finances — one-income budget stability and no credit-card-interest restart.
  4. Career — strong Princess UX execution, senior UX growth, AI/work leverage, and business experiments only when concrete.
  5. Leisure — restoration, hobbies, outings, and future excitement.
  6. Life Admin — immigration/admin/home obligations kept concrete and contained.

Current operating truth

  • TickTick is the execution layer only: concrete tasks, dates, appointments, and protected review sessions.
  • Sapho/check-ins/wiki are the strategy, planning, memory, progress, and advisor layer.
  • Hevy is the gym source of truth; weekly reviews interpret it.
  • The wiki/dashboard should make the operating system legible without becoming another task system.

Next

Current priority

  • Mental-health support profile and practice pipeline upgrade: make mental-health goals/dashboard/planning as clear as the completed physical-health pipeline without feeding overanalysis.

Near-term top 3

  1. Mental-health system: support profile, observer-mode reset script, bounded processing practice, and future-excitement artifact.
  2. Relationships/fatherhood clarity: continue written clarity sessions without circular analysis; use the therapist-guided scenario exercise when ready.
  3. Execution cleanup: keep Princess UX/UI, TickTick, and Sapho system work from turning into admin soup.

Current concrete moves

  • Complete the overdue health baseline actions: book the testosterone/blood-pressure appointment and prepare the lab checklist.
  • Keep the current workday anchored to the payment-page Princess UX task.
  • Use weekly/monthly reviews to turn broad goals into a few weekly commitments, not a larger TickTick inventory.
  • Continue cleaning the continuity control surfaces in this order: open-loops → this page → TickTick action firewall → cron/review overlap audit. The first three are now complete; next is the cron/review overlap audit.

Waiting / Blocked

  • Relationship/fatherhood clarity: waiting on the next written clarity session and/or scenario exercise output.
  • Mental-health support profile: active upgrade now; first-pass cascade lives in mental-health, next missing pieces are helpful/unhelpful responses, warning signs, escalation boundaries, and the first artifact choice.
  • Physical-health targets: system/pipeline marked complete on 2026-05-24; keep under normal weekly/monthly review with medical input/labs and 4–6 weeks of Hevy data before over-calibrating or changing targets.
  • Finance stability: waiting on clean month-to-month budget data and weekly finance review signals.
  • Princess UX organization: waiting on real work-status classification: done, blocked, Jira-only, collapse, archive, or next action.
  • Immigration: USCIS replied; next request is due in about two months unless a non-delay issue appears.
  • Sapho upgrades: roadmap items wait behind open-loop cleanup, strategy compression, and action-firewall work.

Active weekly goal

  • Goal: By 2026-05-31, finish Sapho's Dashboard and SMART Goals work enough that the dashboard shows the current weekly/monthly goals correctly and the active SMART goals are written in a usable, directly checkable form.
  • Progress: 0%
  • Source: Jenffry update on 2026-05-26.
  • Next concrete move: define the remaining dashboard gaps and SMART-goal gaps, then close the smallest visible gap first.

Active monthly goal

  • Goal: By 2026-05-31, establish the clarity-first operating system enough to complete the health baseline loop, keep the one-income finance guardrail intact, and complete at least 2 relationship/fatherhood clarity sessions without circular analysis.
  • Progress: 30%
  • Source: Monthly review.
  • Next concrete move: schedule the next clarity session, finish the health baseline actions, and keep spending inside the guardrail.

SMART goal rule

Active goals should be written as one sentence each with:

  • a specific outcome
  • a measurable proof point or count
  • a clear deadline
  • one next concrete move

Keep the goal short enough to scan quickly. If a goal cannot be made SMART yet, move the missing piece to open-loops instead of padding the goal.

This week's operating rule

One big important task plus one quick win per day.

  • The big task must support a goal, key life domain, or necessary operation.
  • Quick wins are allowed, but they should not crowd out the big task.
  • Normal 9–5 Princess UX/UI work is not personal-planning clutter unless it creates after-hours spillover or strategic risk.
  • If health, family, and work conflict, protect mental health and family first, then choose the minimum viable work/admin move.

TickTick action firewall

No TickTick task should exist just because an idea is interesting.

A task belongs in TickTick only when it has at least one of these:

  • a visible next action
  • a real date/deadline
  • an appointment or event
  • a protected review session that creates output
  • a concrete errand/admin action

Otherwise route it to:

  • open-loops for unresolved questions
  • the relevant area page for durable context
  • sapho-upgrade-roadmap for Sapho/Hermes system upgrades
  • inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md or inbox/perception-log.md for raw capture
  • current chat only if it is not durable

Review cadence

  • Daily morning check-in: choose today’s one big task and one quick win; inspect TickTick enough to avoid overload.
  • Daily evening closeout: land the day, capture slips, set tomorrow’s one big task and one quick win.
  • Weekly review: review commitments, TickTick alignment, Hevy, finance, weekly progress signals, archive candidates, and plan every day of the next week.
  • Monthly review: review the whole goals-to-execution system: long-term bearing, 12-month outcomes, monthly targets, weekly commitments, TickTick alignment, and Sapho process quality.
  • Weekly continuity audit: quietly catch missed durable memory/wiki updates without interrupting unless something is critically broken.

Not the focus right now

  • Generic productivity for its own sake.
  • Endless option collecting.
  • Circular relationship/fatherhood analysis.
  • Saving every detail from every chat.
  • Turning the wiki, dashboard, or TickTick into a giant archive.
  • Building second-order Sapho upgrades before the decision surfaces are clean.
  • Treating task hygiene as sufficient when upstream goals and strategy are unclear.

Deeper pages

  • open-loops — current unresolved loops and pending decisions.
  • decision-log — actual decisions and their implications.
  • sapho-operating-system — how Sapho, wiki, skills, cron, dashboard, and task layers fit together.
  • dashboard — dashboard feed contract and progress-tracking surface.
  • health, mental-health, physical-health — health strategy and body system.
  • relationships — partner/marriage, fatherhood, family, friendships, and social connection.
  • finances — budget and investing context.
  • career, princess-ux — work, senior UX growth, AI leverage, and business direction.
  • leisure — hobbies, outings, restoration, and future excitement.
  • life-admin — immigration, admin, and home obligations.

Preserved source

The previous merged strategy page contained detailed migrated material from current-plan, focus-map, goals-cascade, and life-rebuild. It was preserved before compression at:

  • raw/reference/current-strategy-pre-compression-2026-05-20.md

Future cleanup can progressively distribute that preserved detail into the relevant area pages if useful. Do not re-expand this page unless the top control tower becomes insufficient for real planning.

Dashboard
system/dashboard.md
degree 25

Status: GitHub Pages dashboard opens as Sapho's Dashboard organized into tabs for Goals & Tasks, Health, Finance, Relationships, Career, Leisure, Hedonistic Calculator, Life Admin, Sapho System, and Wiki. Netlify remains a fallback host only. Current priority:

Progress TrackingPerception LogBrain Dump SessionsCurrent StrategyHealthRelationshipsFinancesCareer

Dashboard

Dashboard summary

  • Status: GitHub Pages dashboard opens as Sapho's Dashboard organized into tabs for Goals & Tasks, Health, Finance, Relationships, Career, Leisure, Hedonistic Calculator, Life Admin, Sapho System, and Wiki. Netlify remains a fallback host only.
  • Current priority: keep Goals & Tasks as the default action-oriented command center while giving each major life area, decision aid, and Sapho System its own surface.
  • Active signals: current strategy, active weekly/monthly goal sentences and progress bars, live TickTick dated tasks, goal-progress estimates, SimpleFIN finance cockpit with weekly/monthly category progress, split Health cockpit with mental-health and physical-health panels, Hevy/pickleball/activity rhythm, current strength baseline, the 1-year strength ladder, full body-measurement progress bars, numeric physical-health goal ladder from physical-health.md, Leisure cockpit signals, Hedonistic Calculator client-side scoring, Sapho System operating-state summaries, live active cron inventory, main Sapho skill inventory, SOUL/memory/wiki/system artifact inventory, full wiki graph, and expandable page cards.
  • Open loops: active weekly/monthly goal percentages are provisional until the check-ins define cleaner scoring; the physical-health goal ladder, strength baseline, and body-measurement bars should keep being refined from source data and medical input rather than hand-written dashboard state.
  • Next concrete move: weekly and monthly reviews must set the active goal sentence and progress percentage in current-strategy.md, then the dashboard displays them without becoming the source of truth.

Dashboard is the home for dashboard-generation rules and the dashboard feed contract. It is not the home for duplicated status widgets that belong to other pages.

Dashboard feed contract

The dashboard reads from real wiki pages, not duplicate dashboard-only pages. Each dashboard-fed page should maintain a ## Dashboard summary section.

Dashboard-fed pages should use this shape:

## Dashboard summary

- Status:
- Current priority:
- Active signals:
- Open loops:
- Next concrete move:

Rule: the dashboard should be fed by sections inside real pages, not by a duplicate dashboard-only mini-wiki.

Implication:

  • Progress signals belong in progress-tracking.
  • Perception-pattern output belongs in perception-log.
  • Brain-dump review state belongs in brain-dump-sessions.
  • Life-area status belongs in the relevant area page.
  • Dashboard implementation notes and feed rules belong here.

Dashboard-fed pages currently include:

  • current-strategy
  • health
  • relationships
  • finances
  • career
  • leisure
  • life-admin
  • open-loops
  • decision-log
  • progress-tracking
  • perception-log
  • brain-dump-sessions
  • log

Architecture

  • The dashboard is published on GitHub Pages and renders the generated cockpit plus full wiki graph/source pages.
  • Public URL: https://jenffry90-arch.github.io/sapho-workspace-dashboard/.
  • Fallback URL: https://sapho-workspace-dashboard.netlify.app/.
  • Local generator script: /opt/data/scripts/publish_sapho_dashboard.py.
  • Source-of-truth rule: wiki pages own goals/state, direct source-refresh scripts own mechanical API pulls, the dashboard generator renders/displays, and review jobs interpret trends.
  • Direct source refresh: cron job Daily dashboard source refresh (b7a5b1d24d5c) runs at 09:00, 15:00, and 21:00 UTC (0 9,15,21 * * *) with no_agent=true and local-only delivery. It refreshes dashboard-facing source summaries, syncs the physical-health source page from Hevy body measurements, refreshes the finance cockpit from SimpleFIN, publishes the dashboard to GitHub Pages, and verifies local/live GitHub markers.
  • Standalone publisher: cron job Publish Sapho dashboard (a8af467ed943) previously ran daily at 08:00 UTC via publish_sapho_dashboard_quiet.sh. It is redundant now that the direct source-refresh job publishes and verifies the dashboard; keep it paused unless there is a deliberate reason to publish before API-backed refresh.
  • Keep this as a “map layer,” not a task manager.

What the dashboard renders

The current generator scans the live wiki and renders:

  • tabs: Goals & Tasks, Health, Finance, Relationships, Career, Leisure, Hedonistic Calculator, Life Admin, Sapho System, and Wiki
  • Goals & Tasks tab as the default command center with Strategic top 3, dashboard-backed/provisional goal progress, active weekly/monthly goals, and dated TickTick task windows
  • Health tab with split Mental health and Physical health cockpit panels
  • Finance tab with SimpleFIN budget/cashflow/category cockpit data
  • Relationships, Career, Leisure, and Life Admin tabs with lean life-area summaries sourced from the wiki, with Leisure now expanded into a cockpit for planned anchors, TickTick, hobbies, future excitement, social/leisure, pipeline, bucket list, and guardrails
  • Hedonistic Calculator tab with client-side scoring only; no backend state unless Jenffry explicitly asks to save a result
  • Sapho System tab with architecture cockpit plus operating-layer summaries from strategy, open loops, decisions, dashboard rules, Sapho operating-system pages, all active cron jobs, main active Sapho skills, and non-wiki system artifacts such as SOUL.md, persistent memory/profile, cron registry, publisher script, run ledger, backups, and integrations
  • Wiki tab with wikilink graph, search/filter controls, full page library, expandable page cards, excerpts, and rendered markdown
  • no separate life-area summary block, extra gym/activity card, or daily-prompt card

Non-goals

  • No TickTick write actions.
  • No authentication layer in the current public GitHub Pages setup; do not put secrets or private credentials in the generated dashboard repo.
  • No backup/archive mirror in the main graph yet.
  • No dashboard-only duplicate status widgets for areas that already have real wiki pages.

  • progress-tracking
  • perception-log
  • brain-dump-sessions

Decision Log

Decision Log

This is the living page for "what did we decide?"

Decisions

2026-05-24 — Physical-health pipeline complete; mental health becomes the next build focus

Decision: Treat the physical-health goals, dashboard, and Hevy-backed refresh pipeline as complete enough to move from system-building into normal weekly/monthly review. The next health-domain system build is mental health: goals, dashboard signals, support profile, practice pipeline, and planning cadence.

Why: Jenffry said he is happy with the physical-health SMART goals, automatic Hevy-backed dashboard updates, and overall physical-health system. He wants the rest of the dashboard/life areas brought toward that same level, starting with mental health.

Implementation: Updated mental-health with a first-pass goal cascade, practice pipeline, dashboard/data rules, and next artifact choices; closed the body-system open loop; shifted current strategy toward the mental-health support-profile build.

Related pages:

  • mental-health
  • physical-health
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops
  • progress-tracking

2026-05-22 — Active goals should be written in SMART form

Decision: The active weekly and monthly goals in current-strategy should be written in SMART form: specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound.

Why: Jenffry asked to update the goals to SMART goals. The active goals were still phrased as broad strategy statements, which made them harder to execute and review.

Implementation: Rewrote the active weekly and monthly goal entries in current-strategy with deadlines, measurable proof points, and explicit next moves; added a short SMART goal rule to the same page.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • open-loops

2026-05-20 — Dashboard-fed state belongs to owning pages

Decision: Dashboard-facing widgets must not live in system/dashboard.md as a dashboard-only mini-wiki. Dashboard-facing state belongs inside the real page that owns the domain: progress in progress-tracking, perception patterns in perception-log, brain-dump review state in brain-dump-sessions, and life-area state in the relevant area page.

Why: Jenffry noticed the dashboard page was drifting into a duplicate aggregation page. The intended architecture is that the dashboard renders and summarizes real pages, not that Sapho maintains a second dashboard database.

Implementation: Rewrote dashboard as a feed-contract/governance page, created progress-tracking, added dashboard summaries to perception-log and brain-dump-sessions, strengthened SCHEMA.md, updated cron prompts for quiet perception/brain-dump reviews, and updated related skill references from retired concepts/ progress paths to system/progress-tracking.md.

Related pages:

  • dashboard
  • progress-tracking
  • perception-log
  • brain-dump-sessions
  • SCHEMA

2026-05-20 — Brain dump review runs quietly and feeds dashboard

Decision: The weekly brain dump review should run quietly in the background, review the brain-dump inbox, update status/review summaries in the wiki, and make output visible through the dashboard instead of sending Jenffry a routine Telegram message.

Why: Brain dump review should preserve containment and provide ambient inbox intelligence without creating another user-facing review interruption.

Implementation: Updated cron job Weekly brain dump review (7445bd2463da) to local delivery, rewrote its prompt to update inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md and system/dashboard.md, updated the brain-dump operating rule, and added a dashboard brain-dump review section.

Related pages:

  • brain-dump-sessions

2026-05-20 — Perception pattern review runs quietly and feeds dashboard

Decision: The weekly perception-pattern review should run quietly in the background, analyze the perception-log wiki page, write compact pattern output back to the wiki, and make the output visible through the dashboard instead of sending Jenffry a routine Telegram message.

Why: Jenffry wants the perception-pattern habit to produce useful pattern recognition without becoming another interruptive mental-health check-in or Telegram message.

Implementation: Updated cron job Weekly perception pattern review (1052949b0556) to local delivery, rewrote its prompt to update inbox/perception-log.md and system/dashboard.md, updated the perception-log operating rule, and added a dashboard perception-pattern section.

  • perception-log

2026-05-20 — TickTick action firewall strengthened

Decision: Treat the TickTick action firewall as an explicit operating rule: TickTick gets only executable next actions, real dates/deadlines, appointments/events, protected review sessions that create output, concrete errand/admin actions, or specific waiting/dependency follow-ups.

Why: Jenffry wants TickTick to remain the execution layer, not an idea archive, planning database, or Sapho-system roadmap. This reduces admin soup and makes daily/weekly reviews sharper.

Implementation: Patched the ticktick-task-management skill, added references/ticktick-action-firewall-2026-05-20.md, updated review-system governance so recurring reviews classify questionable tasks through the firewall, and updated current-strategy to mark the first three control-surface cleanup steps complete.

2026-05-18 — Full system restructure around life-area taxonomy

Decision: Implement the final six-area taxonomy across the wiki, goals layer, and TickTick execution layer: Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance.

Why: Jenffry explicitly made the life-area taxonomy the new organizing system across all planning, goals, wiki pages, TickTick projects, and task/folder structure.

Implementation: Renamed/relinked the main wiki life-area pages; created health as the Health hub; reorganized current-strategy, current-strategy, current-strategy, and current-strategy around the six areas; renamed TickTick projects to 🏋‍♂Health, 💞Relationships, 💰Finances, 💼Career, 🎲Leisure, and 🛠Maintenance; moved tasks from old Social/Hobbies/Achievement/Princess/Home/System lists into the matching new projects; deleted emptied old TickTick projects; kept 🗄Archive as the archive list.

Why: Jenffry decided that partner / marriage and fatherhood are the real pillars. The previous support buckets are useful only as content inside those two pillars, not as separate subcategories.

2026-05-18 — Life-area taxonomy finalized as Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance

Decision: Use Health with subcategories mental health and physical health; Relationships with partner / marriage and fatherhood; Finances with budget and investing; Career with business and work; Leisure with social, hobbies, and outings; and Maintenance with no subcategories for now.

Why: Jenffry wanted the final structure simple, short, and usable. He specifically wanted social things separated from Relationships and kept under Leisure, wanted Finance split into budget/investing, wanted Career to stay generic enough for future work, and wanted Maintenance left plain.

Implementation: Updated concepts/life-area-map.md to the final structure and kept immigration/citizenship inside Maintenance when needed.

Why: Jenffry said the link will not be shared, so the dashboard can trade sanitization for legibility. The useful thing now is seeing the whole wiki as connected dots and pages, not just a summary shell.

Implementation: Rewrote /opt/data/scripts/publish_sapho_dashboard.py so it scans the live wiki, builds a wikilink graph, renders every live markdown page as an expandable card, and publishes the updated dashboard back to jenffry90-arch/sapho-workspace-dashboard. The remaining open choice is whether to add the backup/archive mirror into the main view.

2026-05-18 — Perception log is a containment layer like brain dumps

Decision: Treat perception-log as a containment layer for momentary inner-state dumps, similar to brain-dump-sessions. Capture the raw perception first, do not spill it into other pages during capture, and process it later only if Jenffry asks.

Why: Jenffry wants the perception log to work like a brain dump: raw first, processed later, no immediate cross-page synthesis.

Implementation: Updated perception-log and the intake rule in jenffry-continuity so perception logs stay contained until later review or explicit processing.

2026-05-18 — Perception log and weekly pattern review added

Decision: Create perception-log as a lightweight place for Jenffry to dump current inner/body-state perceptions, plus a weekly Sapho review to find patterns, solutions, and insights. The scope is intentionally broader than emotions: body sensations, energy, stress, mood, pain/tension, clarity/fog, urges, sensory impressions, random perceptions, and mixed/unclear signals.

Why: Jenffry wants momentary subjective-state capture without requiring immediate organization or limiting it to emotions. The weekly review gives those entries strategic value by looking for repeated triggers, body/context correlations, protective factors, and practical experiments.

Implementation: Created concepts/perception-log.md and cron job Weekly perception pattern review (1052949b0556) for Friday 5:00 PM America/New_York during EDT (0 21 * * 5 UTC), delivered to Telegram. The review loads jenffry-continuity and health-and-fitness-planning, avoids diagnosis, and keeps outputs compact and practical.

2026-05-18 — Weekly brain dump review keeps dumps contained unless Jenffry asks for processing

Decision: Add a weekly Sapho cron job to review brain-dump-sessions in place, keeping the raw dump contained there unless Jenffry explicitly asks for organization. The job should preserve raw dumps and status markers, but not spill items into other wiki pages or TickTick on its own.

Why: Jenffry wants brain dump sessions to be collected freely first and kept contained until he asks to process them later.

Implementation: Created cron job Weekly brain dump review (7445bd2463da) for Sunday 6:00 PM America/New_York during EDT (0 22 * * 0 UTC), delivered to Telegram. It loads jenffry-continuity, uses gpt-5.4-mini, and can read/write wiki files. It should preserve the raw dump in place and wait for an explicit organization request before moving anything into other pages.

2026-05-17 — Daily/weekly reviews use one-big-task + one-quick-win and feed a progress dashboard

Decision: The weekly review and daily check-ins must both use the same anti-overload rule: one big goal/key-domain/necessary-operation task plus one quick win per day by default. Daily mental-health/tracking questions should feed a lightweight weekly progress dashboard and monthly trend review. Tracking should use a 1–5 scale by default instead of green/yellow/red.

Why: Jenffry wants the planning cascade to be coherent and wants daily tracking to matter later, not disappear into chat. The data should improve weekly/monthly reviews and should be adjustable as the useful metrics become clearer.

Implementation: Created dashboard and dashboard, updated recurring review templates, and updated the scheduled morning, night, weekly, monthly, and Friday mental-health prompts to use the dashboard and 1–5 tracking scale.

2026-05-17 — Sunday weekly review replaces Sunday morning check-in and includes weekend planning

Decision: Keep the daily morning check-in at 5:30 AM ET for Monday through Saturday. On Sunday, replace the separate morning check-in with a combined Sunday 5:30 AM ET weekly review that includes the Sunday planning shape, weekly progress/goal alignment, coming-week commitments, and weekend/social planning. Pause the standalone Tuesday weekend/social planning cron job to avoid duplicate check-ins.

Why: Jenffry wants the morning check-in to support his early gym rhythm. Sunday should be the strategic reset: plan the week and include the weekend/social plan at the same time, rather than adding another separate Sapho message.

Implementation: Updated cron jobs: dc28019ef7c4 now runs Monday-Saturday; 4e51582da395 now runs Sunday 5:30 AM ET as the combined weekly review/morning check-in/weekend plan; 6476c8523f9d is paused; d0cc62903f62 moved to Saturday 10:00 AM ET as a default weekend relationship/fatherhood prompt. Later 2026-05-17 calibration: a42c23ac4083 continuity memory audit now runs as a background/local-output process, and all user-facing check-ins/reviews should include a self-improvement question, tracking questions, and past-facing progress review at the right scale. The weekly review must plan every day of the coming week, including the weekend, with goal-oriented suggestions, pushback, and an explicit anti-overstacking guard. The monthly review must include a long-term planning session before setting monthly targets. Further weekly-review calibration: use clearer top-level sections, make Gym review and Financial review distinct major sections, ignore normal 9–5 Princess UX/UI work in personal weekly planning, and use one big important task plus one quick win per day as the default daily load rule.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops

2026-05-16 — Tokenization is a standing rule for Princess Storybook components

Decision: Every new Princess Storybook component must keep the token layer alive: define or extend semantic component tokens, then make component CSS consume those tokens rather than raw colors, spacing, radii, shadows, or typography values scattered through CSS rules.

Why: Jenffry wants the Storybook to behave like a real design system. If a token changes, the relevant foundations/components should update globally. Hardcoded values make the system brittle and create hidden divergence.

Implementation: Added the rule to plans/princess-storybook-scratch-build.md. Current token structure uses generated foundation tokens in src/styles/tokens.css, semantic tokens in src/styles/semantic-tokens.css, and component groups like --pc-button-*, --pc-field-*, --pc-alert-*, --pc-evidence-*, --pc-viz-*, and --pc-card-*. Exact source values may live in semantic tokens, but should not be scattered through component CSS. Tokenization is part of done for source-backed components.

Related pages:

  • princess-storybook
  • princess-ux

2026-05-14 — Use one-income, no-one-offs budget baseline

Decision: Build Jenffry's household budget from current regular income only: $2,882.97 biweekly net, about $6,246.44/month. Exclude tax refunds, interest, transfers, reimbursements, cash deposits, and other one-off income from everyday budget planning.

Why: A spending-history budget was too loose and distorted by tax refunds and irregular income. Jenffry clarified the household currently has one income, so the budget must be constrained by current regular cashflow.

Implementation: Saved the transaction-analysis summary under raw/finance/budget-rebuild-2026-05-14.md, updated finances with the realistic monthly/weekly budget, and updated open-loops with the verification need around Honda and ongoing weekly finance review.

  • finances
