Decision: Use Jenffry's Mentat personality profile to refine Sapho as disciplined cognition under pressure: human-aware, ethically loyal, dynamic in pattern tracking, calm in delivery, and not an emotionless machine or theatrical character imitation.

Why: Jenffry wants Sapho's personality profile completed so Sapho can function better as strategic advisor and task manager. The document clarified that Mentats are disciplined but human, that emotion and loyalty can bias analysis, and that the useful archetype is a House-Mentat-style guardian mind rather than a twisted or performative Mentat.

Implementation: Preserved the raw document in raw/transcripts/mentat-personality-profile-2026-05-10.md, updated /opt/data/SOUL.md, created sapho-operating-system, updated jenffry, and updated open-loops.

The immigration-case task is the real next action. The phone-safe SSH description is intentionally long because Jenffry can use it independently without asking Sapho to recover the details. Household quick wins are valid but should not scatter across the week.

2026-05-09 — Dedicated TickTick list and wiki page for Princess UX/UI

Decision: Princess UX/UI work gets its own TickTick list, 💼Career, and its own wiki context page, princess-ux.

Why: Princess work has enough active clusters, stakeholder context, due dates, and career/AI leverage to exceed a generic Achievement column. Separating it reduces clutter and makes Sapho support more precise.

Implementation: Created TickTick project/list 💼Career (69ffb6278f0818deaa8b8917) and moved 20 active Princess-related tasks from 💼Career. The wiki page holds context and triage; TickTick holds concrete execution blocks; Jira remains the intended official work source of truth.

Operating rule: Do not create a wiki page for every tiny task. Create dedicated pages for substantial multi-week projects, recurring workstreams, major life areas, or projects with decisions/context/stakeholders that would be costly to reconstruct.

Related pages:

  • princess-ux
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops

2026-05-09 — Add daily evening closeout check-in

Decision: Add a second daily Sapho check-in at night, separate from the morning planning check-in. The evening check-in should close the day, identify what slipped, prompt a small sleep/mental-health reset, include a short Hevy gym signal, and prepare tomorrow's protected moves.

Why: Morning planning alone does not close loops from the actual day. A short night closeout helps salvage messy days, protect sleep, and reduce tomorrow's friction without adding TickTick clutter.

Implementation: Created recurring cron job Daily evening closeout check-in (3c7df597c7a3) and updated it to 8:00 PM America/New_York during the current DST period, updated current-strategy, and patched the TickTick task-management skill with the evening closeout standard.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy
  • physical-health

2026-05-09 — Add Hevy gym progress to Sapho check-ins

Decision: Gym progress should be part of Sapho's progress-tracking layer. Daily check-ins should include a short Hevy gym signal, weekly check-ins should explicitly review the week against the 5-day gym plan, and monthly reviews should include a 30-day body/gym signal.

Why: Jenffry has Hevy Pro connected and the gym/body-system goal needs regular review like other priority areas. Hevy should remain the workout source of truth; TickTick should not become cluttered with gym tracking tasks.

Implementation: Added a progress command to /opt/data/scripts/hevy_api.py; updated the daily, weekly, and monthly cron prompts to load fitness-app-integrations and use Hevy data; and updated current-strategy. Weekly review standard: minimum successful week = Push/Pull/Lower; strong week = minimum plus one posture/cardio day; ideal week = all five Sapho 5-Day Plan routines. Monthly review uses a 30-day Hevy signal for consistency and routine-balance trends.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • physical-health
  • current-strategy

2026-05-09 — Strategic planning layer polish is required for full execution capacity

Decision: Treat strategic-goal and planning-layer polish as a first-class operating-system task, not as optional background cleanup. The goal hierarchy, active priorities, planning cadence, and task-flow rules must be clear enough that TickTick can remain a clean execution layer. The strategic review must explicitly test whether execution tasks support strategic priorities or are turning into admin soup.

Why: Jenffry observed that TickTick execution cannot work at full capacity if the upstream strategy and planning layers are fuzzy. Better task hygiene alone is insufficient; execution needs a polished planning system feeding it. Later refinement: the review must explicitly guard against admin soup — execution tasks that create administrative motion without supporting the strategic layer.

Implementation: Created the high-priority TickTick task Polish strategic goals and planning layers in 🛠Maintenance for Sunday evening, and updated current-strategy and current-strategy.

  • current-strategy

2026-05-09 — TickTick hygiene requires existing labels and weekly archive review

Decision: Every concrete TickTick task should receive appropriate existing labels/tags as part of task hygiene. Sapho should not create new labels unless Jenffry explicitly approves a new taxonomy. Recently archived/completed cleanup items should receive a light review during the weekly check-in so useful ideas or deferred decisions are not lost.

Why: Labels improve execution and retrieval, but inventing new labels creates taxonomy clutter. Archived items are useful as a cleanup mechanism, but some may contain ideas or context that should be revisited, moved to the wiki, or restored only if truly executable.

Implementation: Patched the TickTick task-management skill; updated the daily morning check-in and weekly goals review cron prompts to include label hygiene and archive review.

2026-05-09 — Improve Sapho through Hermes-native layers, not prompt-only tuning

Decision: Sapho should be improved by using the full Hermes operating system: SOUL.md for stable identity, persistent memory for compact durable facts, the wiki for structured continuity, skills for repeatable workflows/templates, cron jobs for reliable check-ins and audits, and tool integrations for real action.

Why: The strongest research pattern is that better assistants come from better context, permissions, feedback loops, and tools — not just better one-off prompts.

Implementation: Updated /opt/data/SOUL.md, patched the jenffry-continuity skill with a Hermes-native improvement loop, saved a compact memory note, and updated current-strategy, sapho-operating-system, and open-loops.

Why: Jenffry wants Sapho to show not only what to do today, but also what is moving, what slipped, and whether the weekly/monthly/6-month goals are receiving concrete action.

Implementation: Updated the daily scheduled check-in prompt so Sapho inspects recent completions when available, tracks momentum/slippage/streak risk, and includes a compact “Progress signal” section.

2026-05-08 — Use Sapho-led goals check-ins, not TickTick check-in tasks

Decision: Track goals in a hierarchy, but run the check-ins through Sapho messages and the continuity wiki rather than recurring TickTick check-in tasks.

Why: Jenffry already has 6-month goals and longer-term placeholders in TickTick, but TickTick should remain an execution layer for concrete things that need to get done. Recurring planning/check-in tasks create bloat. Sapho should provide the advisor layer: review inbox/TickTick, compare today against yesterday and weekly goals, judge whether the plan is bloated, and suggest cuts/moves.

Implementation: Removed the recurring TickTick check-in tasks and created scheduled Sapho check-ins: daily morning goals/execution check-in, weekly goals review, and monthly long-term goals review. Created current-strategy to document the operating model.

Why: Jenffry explicitly identified that memory updates should be automated; relying on memory and habit is too fragile for a continuity system.

Implementation: Created recurring cron job Weekly continuity memory audit (a42c23ac4083) for Sunday evening. The job loads jenffry-continuity, reviews the wiki and recent sessions, updates durable plan/decision/focus/open-loop changes, and reports back to Jenffry.

2026-05-08 — Pursue full-power TickTick task-manager integration

Decision: Sapho should be connected to Jenffry's existing TickTick account with full read/write task access to serve as task manager and advisor through Telegram.

Why: Jenffry wants the best possible setup, not an overly conservative one. TickTick should remain the task source of truth while Sapho supplies triage, planning, prioritization, and execution support.

Implication: Build or connect an integration that can read tasks/projects and create, update, complete, move, schedule, tag, and review tasks. Keep safety focused on intentionality for irreversible or broad changes rather than underpowering Sapho.

Why: Jenffry clarified that the name should sound more appropriate to the Dune universe. Sapho is a Mentat-associated substance in Dune, so it carries the right universe flavor without copying a character identity.

Implementation: Updated /opt/data/SOUL.md, persistent memory, jenffry, and the jenffry-continuity skill. Sapho's formal role remains Jenffry's Mentat; "Hermes" is reserved for the underlying software platform.

2026-05-20 — Continuity cron should maintain current wiki structure

  • Decision: The weekly continuity cron should not only remember missed durable context; it should also clean and upgrade the current wiki after the life-area migration.
  • Reason: Continuity depends on the wiki staying structurally healthy, not just accumulating notes.
  • Implication: The cron checks recent sessions, routes durable signal to system/, areas/, or inbox/, fixes stale retired concepts/ links, keeps index.md and dashboard summaries aligned, and remains local-only unless a critical issue needs attention.

2026-05-20 — Sapho future upgrades get a roadmap

Status: accepted

Context: The uploaded Sapho System Improvement Plan contains many valid future upgrades. Without a home of record, those ideas could scatter across open loops, skills, cron prompts, memory, and chat history.

Decision: Use system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md as the canonical place for future Sapho upgrades. Preserve the raw source document in raw/reference/sapho-system-improvement-plan-2026-05-20.md.

Rationale: The roadmap keeps improvement ideas visible without forcing immediate implementation or creating bloat.

Consequences: Future Sapho upgrade ideas should be routed to the roadmap first, then promoted into implementation plans, skill patches, cron changes, or system pages only when they earn action.

2026-05-20 — Open loops become a status-sorted control surface

Status: accepted

Context: The open-loops page mixed active life loops, system loops, closed history, monitor-only items, and roadmap material.

Decision: Sort open loops by operating status and keep active loops capped at 10. Preserve the pre-cleanup page as raw reference.

Rationale: Sapho needs a clear unresolved-work surface before adding more automation or observability.

Consequences: Weekly/monthly reviews should promote, park, resolve, or retire loops instead of letting them live indefinitely. Sapho upgrade work belongs in system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, not as scattered open-loop clutter.

2026-05-25 — Move Social under Relationships

Decision: Social rebuilding, friendships, invitations, conversation confidence, and social-life planning belong under the Relationships life area, not Leisure.

Why: The main signal is relational health and being seen/connected with people. Leisure still owns hobbies, play, outings, restoration, and future excitement when the main value is fun or hobby momentum.

Operational rule: If the main value is people/contact, route it to Relationships/Social. If the main value is play/restoration/hobby, keep it in Leisure. If both matter, keep the relational thread in Social and the activity/logistics in Leisure only when that separation is useful.

Goals Cascade Template
system/goals-cascade-template.md
degree 8

Use this as the shared shape for turning one life area into a goal cascade. The cascade should move from broad direction to concrete execution: long-term = direction yearly = milestone 6-month = checkpoint monthly = SMART target weekly = SMART execution target

Open LoopsHealthRelationshipsFinancesCareerLeisureLife Admin

Goals Cascade Template

Use this as the shared shape for turning one life area into a goal cascade.

Purpose

The cascade should move from broad direction to concrete execution:

  • long-term = direction
  • yearly = milestone
  • 6-month = checkpoint
  • monthly = SMART target
  • weekly = SMART execution target
  • daily = one concrete next move

Do not make the top layers fake-precise. Keep them directional and measurable enough to review. Put missing uncertainty or unresolved details in open-loops instead of padding the goal.

Generic template

1) Long-term goal

Time horizon: 3–5 years, or whatever “long-term” means for this area

Template:

Over the long term, I want to [directional outcome] so that [why it matters].

Success looks like: [2–4 broad markers].

2) Yearly goal

Time horizon: 12 months

Template:

By [date], I will [yearly milestone] as shown by [measurable proof].

3) 6-month goal

Time horizon: 6 months

Template:

By [date], I will [midpoint milestone] measured by [proof].

4) Monthly goal

Time horizon: 1 month

Template:

By [date], I will [specific outcome] measured by [count / result / proof].

5) Weekly goal

Time horizon: 1 week

Template:

This week, I will [specific weekly action] as shown by [weekly proof].

6) Daily goal

Time horizon: today

Template:

Today, I will [one concrete action] that moves the weekly goal forward.

SMART rule for the lower layers

Use full SMART precision for monthly and weekly goals:

  • Specific — the outcome is clear
  • Measurable — there is proof or a count
  • Achievable — it is realistic right now
  • Relevant — it supports the life area and current strategy
  • Time-bound — it has a deadline

Fill-order rule

When building a life area, fill it in this order:

  1. Long-term direction
  2. Yearly milestone
  3. 6-month checkpoint
  4. Monthly SMART goal
  5. Weekly SMART goal
  6. Daily move

If the lower level is unclear, stop and clarify before inventing detail.

Life-area application

Use the same cascade for each life area:

  • health
  • relationships
  • finances
  • career
  • leisure
  • life-admin

Each area can have its own version of the same structure, but the shape stays consistent so the full strategy can cascade cleanly from direction to action.

Example sentence form

A strong goal often reads like:

By [date], I will [specific action or outcome] as shown by [measure], because it supports [why it matters].

That sentence is the basic shape for the monthly and weekly layers.

  • Active signals: relationship/fatherhood clarity, mental-health support profile, body system, finance stability, Princess UX organization, execution-layer hygiene, immigration follow-up, social rebuild, cardio routine, and career/business experiments.

Cleanup note

This page was restructured on 2026-05-20. The full pre-cleanup source is preserved at:

Active Open Loops — max 10

Relationship/fatherhood clarity

Status: open — practice layer defined

Question: What observations, questions, scenarios, possible futures, boundaries, regrets to avoid, and next actions would create real clarity without circular analysis?

Status update: 2026-05-11 intake clarified that the 12-month goal cannot be fully defined in advance. Success means achieving clarity and being comfortable with it, whatever the final family/relationship structure is. The practice layer is written clarity sessions: answer prompts, play out futures, test what acting kindly/not abandoning himself means, identify regrets to avoid, protect Nolan from chaos, and choose one next observation/action.

Suggested next action: Complete the TickTick Relationship/fatherhood clarity session; use the new weekly Sapho clarity prompt to make the work recurring without turning the goal structure into TickTick bloat.

Prepare Sapho for mental-health support role

Status: open — active upgrade in progress

Question: What should Sapho know about Jenffry's mental-health issues, warning signs, helpful responses, unhelpful responses, boundaries, and escalation rules?

Status update: 2026-05-11 intake captured observer mode, acceptance/commitment toward current life, and fear/acceptance around possible recurrence of the late-January weed incident.

Status update: 2026-05-24 Jenffry marked the physical-health goals/dashboard/pipeline complete and chose mental health as the next area to bring to the same clarity level. mental-health now has a SMART cascade built around fear as the core mechanism and the strategy: ignore, redirect, accept, and embrace.

Status update: 2026-05-25 TickTick mental-health tracking was finalized as 15 habit trackers: Stop checking, Therapy, Physical activity, Healthy diet, Walk, no phone, Social outing, Mood, Sleep, Anxiety, Energy, Relax, Leisure activity, Life story, Self reflect, and Meditate. Anxiety uses Option B: 1 = poor anxiety control, 5 = strong anxiety control. Habit Log notes are written to /opt/data/wiki/private/health/mood-tracking-notes.md and intentionally included in the public dashboard.

Suggested next action: Use the 15-habit tracker for one week, then review which signals are useful versus noisy/admin-heavy.

Finance stability

Status: open — income-based budget created; verification and execution remain

Question: Can the household budget stay stable for a clean month without tax refund or one-off income distortion, without credit-card interest restarting, and with enough monthly excess/enjoyment money to stop feeling purely survival-based?

Status update: 2026-05-11 finance goal pass clarified that savings are strong: about $36k emergency fund and over $500k in 401k. The real issue is monthly budget comfort and cashflow.

Status update: 2026-05-14 transaction review rebuilt the budget using Jenffry's corrected income: $2,882.97 biweekly net, about $6,246.44/month. The new working budget excludes tax refunds/one-offs and shows the core constraint: JPMorgan + TD debt/loan assumptions consume about $3,962.98/month, leaving about $2,283.46/month or about $525.54/week for non-debt spending. See finances.

Suggested next action: Continue weekly Finance review; compare spending against the income-based caps, keep May controlled, verify whether Honda truly stopped after the April payoff-sized payment, and refine the path toward ~$400/month comfortable excess.

Princess UX/UI work organization

Status: open — success buckets defined

Question: What structure will let Sapho help Jenffry organize Princess UX/UI work without duplicating Jira or creating TickTick admin soup, while supporting senior UX growth?

Status update: 2026-05-11 goals-layer pass defined five Princess UX/career success buckets: work quality, execution, influence, portfolio/visible proof, and AI/work leverage. The next need is selecting 2–3 concrete proof projects/case-study candidates and a weekly practice/review cadence.

Status update: 2026-05-14 operating-system cleanup moved the overdue Princess UX/UI dated pile out of May 13 and spread it across Fri May 15, Mon May 18, and Tue May 19. No Princess tasks were deleted or archived; this was a reversible overload-clearing pass. Remaining issue: confirm which items are already done, blocked, should move to Jira, or should be collapsed/archived after Jenffry reviews real work status.

Suggested next action: Use princess-ux as the context layer. Keep Jira as the work source of truth, TickTick as dated execution blocks, and Sapho/wiki for synthesis. First clarify the official Jira structure and highest-stakes Princess clusters, then organize work into clarify/design/review/handoff/systemize buckets. Treat AI workflow as a bounded real-work experiment, not an abstract project.

Strategic review: execution layer versus strategic layer

Status: open

Question: Is the current TickTick execution layer genuinely supporting the strategic layer, or has it become admin soup?

Status update: Stage A executed on 2026-05-10. The Princess UX + AI workflow cluster was moved to 🗄Archive; the phone-safe SSH task was intentionally left long because Jenffry wants the self-service details in TickTick; the broad Become a US Citizen task was moved to 🗄Archive; and home/family quick wins should be batched in evening blocks.

Status update: 2026-05-25 dashboard polish clarified the next goals-system improvement: future weekly/monthly SMART goals should have automatic tracking sources when possible, such as TickTick task completions, habits, or dashboard source data, instead of hardcoded progress percentages.

Suggested next action: Continue protecting the execution layer against admin soup during weekly reviews. Future cleanup should focus on newly accumulated abstract/system-building tasks, broad goals without next actions, excessive household quick wins outside evening batches, and hardcoded goals that need real tracking signals.

Immigration and citizenship path

Status: open — USCIS replied; next request due in about 2 months

Question: What is actionable for Jenffry's immigration case after more than two years pending, and what is the longer citizenship path after that?

Current 12-month definition: green card if the process allows it; otherwise no passive waiting — confirm case status, normal processing time, documents/notices, escalation options, and take the reasonable next action available.

Status update: USCIS replied by email. Jenffry now needs to submit another request in about 2 months rather than waiting on the old follow-up window.

Suggested next action: Keep the broad citizenship goal in life-admin, not as an active TickTick container task. Preserve the email reply and set the next USCIS request for about 2 months out. If a non-delay issue appears — lost document, USCIS error, missed notice, approved expedite stuck, or urgent humanitarian issue — consider earlier Ombudsman/attorney/congressional escalation.

Social rebuild

Status: open — 12-month markers partly defined

Question: How can Jenffry protect hobbies, weekends, and future excitement so leisure restores agency rather than becoming avoidance, while social rebuilding lives under Relationships?

Status update: 2026-05-25 taxonomy correction: social rebuilding, friendships, invitations, and social confidence now belong under relationships / social. Leisure still tracks hobbies, family/solo outings, restoration, and future excitement. Older 2026-05-11 markers about friends/social outings should be interpreted as Relationships/Social markers, not Leisure markers.

Suggested next action: Use leisure for one concrete hobby/restoration activity at a time and social for one concrete social experiment at a time. TickTick should get only concrete outcomes, not recurring planning clutter.

Status: open

Question: How should Jenffry's workout routines change so cardio days are clearer, more usable, and better aligned with the current health/body goals?

Suggested next action: Review the current workout routine and decide what the cardio days should actually contain, how often they should happen, and how they fit around lifting, pickleball, and recovery.

Business/career direction

Status: open — success buckets drafted

Status update: 2026-05-11 goals-layer pass set this area to follow a Princess-like bucket structure: concrete experiments, work leverage, business-development option value, visible proof/assets, and bandwidth/mental-health fit. Blaze remains attractive but inactive unless experiments show demand, capability, and bandwidth.

Suggested next action: Choose 1–3 small experiments or proof assets related to AI leverage, senior UX growth, leadership, and possible business directions using career.

Suggested next action: Use the scenario set in the next relationship clarity session to make tradeoffs visible instead of keeping the question abstract.

Formalize Sapho model-routing policy matrix

Status: open — return later

Question: Should Sapho create an explicit model-routing matrix before considering any custom auto-router, covering job/session type, default model, allowed fallback, forbidden downgrade, review cadence, and reason?

Status update: 2026-05-17 discussion concluded that the first step should be a visible policy-based routing matrix rather than jumping to OpenRouter auto-router or a custom live model router. Candidate rows include live mental health/relationship/family, monthly goals/full weekly review, evening closeout, memory/lean audits, mechanical status checks, and unknown/default routing.

Suggested next action: Build the matrix, audit all cron jobs against it, and only then decide whether to add a cron-only policy enforcer or a true live message router.

Define Sapho permissions and recurring templates

Status: partly closed on permissions; templates still open

Permission decision: Jenffry wants Sapho to have full permissions by default. Permissions are not the blocker. Sapho should act with disciplined judgment, especially around broad/destructive/sensitive actions, but should not treat lack of permission as a reason for passivity.

Template calibration rule: every recurring check-in should include a brief question asking how to improve that check-in. Use the answers to patch skills/templates and reduce noise over time.

Suggested next action: Continue calibrating output templates from real check-ins. Morning check-in is now Monday-Saturday at 5:30 AM ET; Sunday 5:30 AM ET is a combined weekly review, Sunday morning check-in, and weekend/social plan. All user-facing recurring check-ins should include a self-improvement question, tracking questions, and progress review at the right scale: light daily signal, closeout slip/move signal, weekly review, monthly strategic review. Weekly planning must cover every day of the coming week, including weekend, while using goal-oriented suggestions, pushback, and anti-overstacking guards. Monthly review must include a long-term planning session before setting monthly targets. Current weekly-review template should use clearer top-level sections; make Gym review and Financial review distinct major sections; ignore normal 9–5 Princess UX/UI work in personal weekly planning; and use one big important task plus one quick win per day as the default daily load rule. The continuity memory audit should stay background/local-only unless a critical issue needs direct attention.

Build Sapho workspace blueprint / internal-systems dashboard

Status: active — full wiki browser added to the hosted dashboard; backup/archive mirror still undecided

Question: How should Sapho make the continuity/wiki/skills/cron/TickTick system legible as a folder/workspace-style map, so Jenffry can see the whole operating system without relying on hidden context?

Status update: 2026-05-18 comparison against the folder/workspace video found strong alignment in philosophy — markdown continuity, separated context, reusable skills, and routing AI to the right files — but a clear weakness in Sapho's legibility. Current routing is split across wiki pages, skills, cron prompts, memory, scripts, and TickTick. The missing layer is an at-a-glance workspace blueprint or dashboard showing life-area rooms, raw capture containers, review cadences, routing rules, and system health.

Status update: Initial blueprint page now exists as sapho-operating-system with room map, routing rules, and weekly review touchpoints.

Status update: A concrete implementation page now exists as dashboard to guide the first web dashboard build.

Status update: The hosted dashboard now renders the full live wiki with a graph view and page browser. The remaining question is whether to add the backup/archive mirror into the main view or keep the graph focused on the live wiki only.

Suggested next action: Decide whether to add a backup/archive toggle or keep the live wiki graph uncluttered.

Needs Information

Capture January mental-health incident context

Status: partly open — sensitive/foundational

Question: What happened during the January weed-related mental-health incident, and what context does Sapho need to better support Jenffry's mental-health planning and conversations?

Status update: 2026-05-11 intake captured the current meaning for goals work: Jenffry is afraid it can happen again, is working on accepting that recurrence is possible, and wants to commit that if it happens again he can work through it because he survived it once.

Suggested next action: If/when useful, capture fuller incident context, warning signs, helpful responses, unhelpful responses, and escalation boundaries. Sapho should preserve useful context without treating itself as a therapist or medical authority.

Testosterone and lipid follow-up

Status: open

Question: What are the cardiologist/endocrinologist next steps, and what testosterone plan makes sense with medical input?

Suggested next action: Prepare questions for medical appointments; do not treat AI as medical authority.

Review gpt-5.4-mini quality for background continuity/system audits

Status: open — monitor after next runs

Question: Does running the weekly continuity memory audit and weekly Sapho lean-system audit on openai-codex / gpt-5.4-mini preserve enough judgment quality, or does it create missed context, shallow recommendations, bad pruning, or operating-system drift?

Status update: 2026-05-17 model-routing decision set both jobs to gpt-5.4-mini to preserve gpt-5.5 capacity while keeping important background audits inside the Codex/OpenAI family. This is a deliberate trial, not a final settled routing rule.

Suggested next action: After the next 1–2 runs, inspect the local cron outputs for quality: whether memory recommendations are conservative and useful, whether lean-system audit avoids bloat and false optimizations, and whether either job should move back to gpt-5.5 or to DeepSeek V4 Pro.

Parked / Later

Solidify Jenffry's goals layer

Status: open — first pass complete; execution/refinement remains

Question: What are Jenffry's current goals, and how should they cascade from long-term direction to 12-month outcomes, 6-month goals, monthly targets, weekly commitments, and concrete TickTick tasks?

Why it matters: Sapho's core roles are strategic advisor and task manager. Those roles require a reliable model of Jenffry's goals, capabilities, limitations, and context. The current goal information is incomplete, which weakens advice and task management.

Status update: 2026-05-10 voice intake captured the first 12-month goals layer by life area. 2026-05-11 voice intake refined the mental-health goal into mental-health, completed first-pass definitions across the earlier eight-domain draft in current-strategy, and added a Hevy-based physical-health baseline/target page. 2026-05-18 restructure collapsed the goals layer into the final six life areas: Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance.

Suggested next action: Treat broad goal definition as good enough for this pass. Next work is execution and calibration: monthly targets, weekly commitments, TickTick alignment, and lightweight progress signals without creating admin soup.

Define concrete 12-month goal metrics

Status: open — first pass complete; calibration remains

Question: What concrete measures define the 12-month goals so they are not fuzzy?

Status update: first-pass observable 12-month markers now exist across the final six life areas in current-strategy. Health contains mental-health and physical-health markers; Relationships contains partner / marriage, fatherhood, and social connection practice-layer structures; Finances contains budget and investing; Career contains work and business; Leisure contains hobbies, outings, restoration, and future excitement; Maintenance contains concrete admin/immigration follow-through.

Suggested next action: Convert the first-pass goals into monthly/weekly progress signals without tracking bloat. Recalibrate physical-health targets after 4–6 weeks of Hevy data on the Sapho plan; refine finance comfort, Princess proof projects, career/business experiments, work-relationship target, and mental-health support boundaries during reviews.

Create a bucket list

Status: open

Question: What experiences, trips, hobbies, milestones, family memories, social adventures, and personal achievements does Jenffry want to make room for over the next several years?

Why it matters: A bucket list can strengthen the Leisure and future-excitement layer without turning everything into productivity. It should help identify what is actually worth protecting time, money, and planning energy for.

Suggested next action: Do a first-pass capture session: collect ideas without filtering, then sort them into near-term, someday, family, solo, social, travel, hobby/skill, and achievement-style items. Only turn specific chosen items into TickTick tasks when they are ready to become concrete plans.

Complete Sapho personality profile

Status: partly complete — first personality profile ingested on 2026-05-10

Question: What additional persona details should shape Sapho's tone, judgment, boundaries, and working style?

Status update: Jenffry sent a Mentat personality profile. Sapho preserved the raw document, updated /opt/data/SOUL.md, created sapho-operating-system, and recorded the applied model: disciplined cognition under pressure, not emotionless-machine behavior; ethical loyalty; dynamic pattern tracking; awareness of emotional bias.

Suggested next action: Keep this loop open only for additional Jenffry-specific Sapho behavior preferences, not for more Dune lore unless it changes actual operating behavior.

Continue learning Jenffry's operating profile

Status: open — ongoing

Question: What information about Jenffry's capabilities, limitations, constraints, preferences, energy patterns, relationships, work context, and decision patterns would make Sapho a better strategic advisor and task manager?

Suggested next action: Keep a standing, low-clutter intake loop. Save durable signal when it improves future advice or execution; avoid saving noisy one-off details.

System Open Loops

Sapho operating-system improvement review

Status: active — first lean/bloat pass completed 2026-05-17

Question: Is Sapho's layer above the code — persona, memory, wiki, skills, scheduled check-ins, TickTick hygiene, and review cadence — reducing fog and increasing agency, or creating its own admin load?

Current assessment: The architecture is strong and now has a lean-governance layer. The first bloat pass found the main risk in the skills reference layer and recurring cron prompt length, not in the wiki itself. Sapho consolidated overlapping TickTick review-calibration references into ticktick-task-management/references/review-system-governance-2026-05-17.md, kept the canonical review-templates-2026-05-17.md, removed absorbed reference files, shortened user-facing review cron prompts to point at canonical templates/governance, removed the paused duplicate weekend/social cron job, and created weekly local-only lean audit job 292f4883ad21.

Suggested next action: Let the weekly lean audit run quietly. It should only compress or remove when durable context is preserved and the change is likely to improve token usage, response speed, prompt size, or recurring-output clarity. Continue staged TickTick overload cleanup separately; do not let lean governance become another user-facing admin process.

Define what belongs in the wiki

Status: open

Question: What exact kinds of personal/work information should Hermes save into the wiki versus short-term chat or built-in memory?

Suggested next action: As real conversations happen, save only things that improve continuity, clarity, or action.

Monitor Only

Email inbox integration for morning check-ins

Status: restored on 2026-05-17 — monitor only

Result so far: Google Workspace OAuth was connected on 2026-05-08 for Jenffry's Google account with Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Contacts scopes. Gmail search and Calendar listing were verified, and the daily morning check-in job includes inbox and calendar attention.

Temporary issue: during the early 2026-05-17 morning check-in, Google Workspace auth returned TOKEN_REVOKED / invalid_grant: Token has been expired or revoked. Later the same morning, running the Google Workspace commands with the known dependency prefix (PYTHONPATH=/opt/data/google-python-deps) showed AUTHENTICATED, and Gmail unread search plus Calendar listing worked again.

Suggested next action: No re-auth needed unless the failure recurs. If a future check-in reports Google failure, first retry with the dependency prefix before assuming OAuth is revoked.

Configure OpenRouter fallback provider

Status: closed on 2026-05-16

Question: Should Sapho use OpenRouter as a backup provider when the primary OpenAI Codex route is unavailable/rate-limited?

Result: OpenRouter fallback and direct DeepSeek auxiliary models are configured. /opt/data/.env contains both OPENROUTER_API_KEY and DEEPSEEK_API_KEY without exposing values; /opt/data/config.yaml has fallback_providers set to OpenRouter using deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro; direct DeepSeek auxiliary compression, session-search, and title-generation use deepseek-v4-pro. Live tests returned OPENROUTER_DEEPSEEK_V4_PRO_OK for OpenRouter and DIRECT_DEEPSEEK_V4_PRO_OK for direct DeepSeek; the gateway is running after the configured state was present, so Telegram sessions should have the updated model routing available.

Pricing decision: DeepSeek direct V4 Pro and OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro show the same headline discounted prices for cache-miss input and output ($0.435/M input, $0.87/M output), so Jenffry's rule selects OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro as the fallback. Direct DeepSeek exposes an explicit cache-hit discount, so direct remains useful for auxiliary summarization/compression where already configured.

Suggested next action: Monitor the next real OpenAI Codex rate-limit event. If fallback behavior does not trigger as expected, inspect gateway logs for provider routing/fallback errors and adjust the fallback model list.

Retired / Closed

Body system / physical-health pipeline

Status: closed on 2026-05-24 — completed and moved to monitoring

Result: Jenffry marked the physical-health goals, physical-health dashboard, and whole physical-health system/pipeline complete. The canonical source is physical-health, Hevy remains the source of truth, and the dashboard/direct refresh pipeline now maintains the live physical-health display. Remaining physical-health work is normal execution, weekly/monthly review, medical input, and later calibration — not an open system-build loop.

Move goal structure out of TickTick if it creates bloat

Status: closed on 2026-05-09

Result: Jenffry approved archiving the 15 planning records from the 🎯Goals TickTick list, moving Finance review to 💰Finances, moving Relationship/fatherhood clarity session to 🏋‍♂Health as the mental-health/clarity home, and deleting the empty 🎯Goals TickTick container. Long-term and 6-month goal structure now belongs in the wiki/check-ins, not TickTick.

Verify self-improving skill notes loop

Status: closed on 2026-05-17 — inspected

Question: Is Hermes Agent's self-improving skill loop actually capturing useful notes from repeated review/check-in runs and turning them into better skills/templates, or is Sapho only relying on memory/habit?

Finding: The loop is working in-turn. Recent check-in/review feedback produced concrete updates to governing skills, reusable templates, cron prompts, wiki decisions/open loops/logs, and concise reference files. Evidence includes same-day patches to jenffry-continuity, ticktick-task-management, personal-finance-budgeting, health-and-fitness-planning, and weekend-social-planning, plus the reusable review templates and tracking dashboard calibration.

Weakness: The loop is not yet fully autonomous or lean. It depends on Sapho recognizing reusable corrections during the turn, and the growing number of reference files/prompts could itself become bloat.

Result: Original inspection loop closed. Carry forward the governance problem into the Sapho-system bloat/lean review: keep the improvement loop, but audit whether skill/reference/template additions should be merged, shortened, or pruned.

Build the habit of updating memory

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: Replaced reliance on habit with a scheduled weekly continuity memory audit cron job.

TickTick integration

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: TickTick OAuth credentials were stored in /opt/data/.env, authorization completed with tasks:read tasks:write, project listing was verified, and write access was verified by creating and completing a harmless test task in 🛠Maintenance. Sapho can now read and write TickTick tasks through Telegram.

Intake Jenffry's initial info dump

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: Raw source saved and synthesized into the wiki's core continuity pages plus new topic pages.

Create the continuity operating skill

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: Created custom Hermes skill jenffry-continuity to guide when and how to use the wiki for ongoing memory.

Set up the wiki structure

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: Wiki created at /opt/data/wiki with starter folders and rule files.

Progress Tracking
system/progress-tracking.md
degree 10

Status: Active lightweight tracking page for daily signals and weekly trend summaries. Current priority: keep tracking useful without feeding rumination or admin soup. Active signals: sleep, capacity / mental-health state, observer-mode / rumination, presence

DashboardMental HealthPhysical HealthFinancesCurrent Strategy

Progress Tracking

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active lightweight tracking page for daily signals and weekly trend summaries.
  • Current priority: keep tracking useful without feeding rumination or admin soup.
  • Active signals: sleep, capacity / mental-health state, observer-mode / rumination, presence / future excitement, gym intention/completion, finance guardrail, relationship/fatherhood clarity, career proof, leisure/social restoration, life-admin follow-through, make-it-count outcome. Mental-health dashboard scores now use TickTick habit values where available.
  • Open loops: summarize the current week during weekly review, display trend infographics on the dashboard, and adjust prompts if signals are noisy or not useful.
  • Next concrete move: keep daily tracking compact, use weekly review for broader area signals, and let the dashboard show the tracking cockpit instead of duplicating status by hand.

This page is the home for daily/weekly progress signals. The dashboard should read this page rather than storing progress widgets inside system/dashboard.md.

Operating rule

Daily check-ins may ask for short tracking answers. Those answers should feed the weekly dashboard and monthly review. If a question is not useful, it should be removed or replaced during reviews.

Use a 1–5 scale by default instead of green/yellow/red:

  • 1 = poor / high friction / needs protection
  • 2 = weak / strained
  • 3 = workable / neutral
  • 4 = good / stable
  • 5 = strong / notably good

When the metric is a negative signal, label it clearly so the scale is not confusing. Example: “observer-mode intensity: 1 low, 5 high.”

Tracking framework

Tracking has two layers:

  1. Daily minimum signal — only 1–3 prompts in a check-in, usually sleep/capacity plus one current-focus signal.
  2. Weekly area signal — broader life-area measures summarized during weekly review and displayed as infographics on the dashboard.

Daily signal set

Use these when they are relevant to the day:

  1. Sleep quality: 1–5, optionally hours if Jenffry gives them.
  2. Capacity / mental-health state: 1–5.
  3. Observer-mode / rumination intensity: 1–5, where 1 = low and 5 = high.
  4. Presence / future-excitement signal: 1–5.
  5. Gym intention/completion: planned, completed, skipped, or substituted.
  6. Finance guardrail: stayed within today’s guardrail, yes/no/unclear.
  7. Make-it-count outcome: the one result that would make today count.

Weekly life-area signal set

Use these during weekly review or dashboard synthesis, not as daily homework:

  • Health: mental-health split from physical-health; sleep trend; capacity; rumination/observer-mode; presence/future-excitement; gym sessions; pickleball/activity substitution; cardio/mobility; weight/waist/abdomen measurements; strength-goal anchors; medical/lab follow-through.
  • Relationships: relationship/fatherhood clarity session progress, fatherhood presence, partner/marriage repair/strain signal, avoided circular analysis.
  • Finances: weekly spending guardrail, surplus/deficit comfort, debt/loan autopay status, no-credit-card-interest guardrail.
  • Career: Princess UX proof/workstream progress, senior-UX practice, AI/work leverage experiment, work spillover risk.
  • Leisure: social contact, planned outing, hobby/future-excitement action, restoration rather than avoidance.
  • Life Admin: immigration waypoint, appointments, home/admin obligations, unresolved paperwork.
  • Sapho system: dashboard freshness, open-loop load, wiki/source-of-truth cleanliness, cron/backup health.

Dashboard infographic display rules

The dashboard should render tracking as a cockpit, not a second database:

  • pull daily trend numbers from this page’s Daily log
  • pull area status from each page’s own ## Dashboard summary
  • pull unresolved-load distribution from open-loops.md
  • use bars/rings/coverage cards for quick scanning
  • keep the raw detail inside the owning wiki pages

Active daily tracking prompts

Keep the daily set small. Ask only 1–3 of these when relevant:

  1. Sleep quality: 1–5, optionally hours if Jenffry gives them.
  2. Capacity / mental-health state: 1–5.
  3. Observer-mode / rumination intensity: 1–5, where 1 = low and 5 = high.
  4. Presence / future-excitement signal: 1–5.
  5. Gym intention/completion: planned, completed, skipped, or substituted.
  6. Finance guardrail: stayed within today’s guardrail, yes/no/unclear.
  7. Make-it-count outcome: the one result that would make today count.

Daily questions should be adjusted from the active weekly/monthly focus. Do not ask all metrics every day.

Current week

Week: 2026-05-25 to 2026-05-31

Active tracking focus

  • Daily 1–5 + note: Stop checking, Healthy diet, Mood, Sleep, Anxiety, Energy.
  • Daily note: Walk, no phone, Self reflect.
  • Weekly note, at least once/week: Therapy, Physical activity, Social outing, Leisure activity, Life story.
  • Practice frequency + note: Relax at least 3/week; Meditate at least 5/week.
  • Anxiety control uses Option B: 1 = poor anxiety control, 5 = strong anxiety control.
  • Habit Log notes are written to /opt/data/wiki/private/health/mood-tracking-notes.md and intentionally included in the public dashboard.
  • Gym intention/completion: planned, completed, skipped, substituted.
  • Finance guardrail: stayed within guardrail, yes/no/unclear.
  • Make-it-count outcome: one result for the day.

These stay active for one more week because they produced useful signal without too much tracking load.

Daily log

Use one compact row per day. Do not overfill this page.

  • Monday: pending.
  • Tuesday: 2026-05-26 — TickTick reviewed; day felt overloaded/partial with overdue spillover; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's health baseline appointment and keep tomorrow to one big task plus one quick win; note: mental-health and finance signals not directly reported.
  • Wednesday: pending.
  • Thursday: pending.
  • Friday: pending.
  • Saturday: pending.
  • Sunday: pending.

Suggested entry format:

  • YYYY-MM-DD — TickTick mental-health habits updated; gym ___; finance ___; make-it-count: ___; note: ___

Weekly trend summary

Previous week, 2026-05-18 to 2026-05-24:

  • Sleep trend: mostly workable, with 3–4/5 entries; not enough full daily data for a clean trend.
  • Capacity / mental-health trend: stable-to-workable; one low-capacity rest day was handled as recovery rather than failure.
  • Observer-mode / rumination trend: low-to-moderate where recorded, but incomplete data.
  • Gym trend: strong week from Hevy — 4 workouts, 68 working sets, average 51.1 minutes; missed ideal 5-day plan but exceeded the minimum.
  • Finance guardrail trend: strained — week non-debt spending reached 82% of the $525.54 cap by the latest 2026-05-22 transaction data; monthly usable remaining is negative.
  • Goal-alignment trend: health and body-system execution moved; finance and relationship/fatherhood clarity need tighter protection next week.
  • Main lesson for next week: physical-health system work is complete enough to move into normal review; next strategic build is the mental-health support profile, observer-mode reset, and future-excitement/practice pipeline while keeping tracking light.

Weekly review responsibilities

Weekly reviews should:

  • summarize the daily signals from this page or recent check-in notes
  • identify trend direction: improving, stable, worsening, noisy, or insufficient data
  • connect trends to weekly goal alignment and next-week planning
  • update the active tracking prompts if a question is not producing useful signal
  • keep the one-big-task + one-quick-win rule visible in the coming week plan

Monthly review responsibilities

Monthly reviews should:

  • summarize weekly trends across the month
  • identify whether tracked signals support or threaten long-term goals
  • decide which metrics to keep, drop, add, or change for the next month
  • avoid over-measuring mental health in a way that feeds rumination

Anti-bloat guard

If tracking starts feeling like homework, reduce it. A useful default is one sleep/capacity question plus one focus-specific question per day, then weekly/monthly synthesis.

Run Ledger / Flight Recorder
system/run-ledger.md
degree 1

Status: Initial run ledger design and lightweight writer exist; automatic logging is not yet enabled. Current priority: make meaningful Sapho actions traceable without logging noisy every-token/every-tool detail. Active signals: schema lives in /opt/data/logs/

No resolved wikilinks.

Run Ledger / Flight Recorder

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Initial run ledger design and lightweight writer exist; automatic logging is not yet enabled.
  • Current priority: make meaningful Sapho actions traceable without logging noisy every-token/every-tool detail.
  • Active signals: schema lives in /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/schema-v1.json; writer lives at /opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py; runtime entries live under /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/entries/.
  • Open loops: use the writer on major action runs, decide dashboard health summary, and connect cron failure notes only after the schema proves useful.
  • Next concrete move: create a few real run entries for major wiki/skill/cron/TickTick changes, then decide whether automation would reduce confusion.

Purpose

The run ledger is Sapho's flight recorder.

It should answer, in plain English:

  • What was Jenffry asking for?
  • How did Sapho classify the request?
  • What context and skills were loaded?
  • What tools were used?
  • What files, cron jobs, memories, tasks, or messages changed?
  • What was verified?
  • Was anything committed, pushed, sent, scheduled, or left uncommitted?
  • What errors or approval boundaries appeared?

The goal is trust and debuggability, not surveillance or admin bloat.

What gets logged

Log only meaningful runs, such as:

  • wiki, skill, cron, memory, TickTick, dashboard, or eval changes
  • commits, pushes, publishes, external sends, or scheduled jobs
  • destructive or high-risk operations
  • failed runs that need debugging
  • model/tool-routing decisions that may affect quality
  • user corrections that reveal a system-behavior issue

Do not log:

  • ordinary chat with no durable action
  • every internal reasoning step
  • every small read-only lookup
  • secrets, tokens, OAuth files, API keys, or private credentials
  • raw emotional/perception dumps unless Jenffry explicitly asks to process them

Canonical homes

  • Human-readable policy: system/run-ledger.md.
  • Runtime entries: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/entries/.
  • Machine-readable schema: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/schema-v1.json.
  • Roadmap status: system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md.
  • Routing rule: system/source-of-truth-registry.md.

Entry shape

Each run ledger entry should be one JSON file with these sections:

  • run_id — stable identifier, usually timestamp plus short slug.
  • started_at / ended_at — ISO timestamps.
  • source — Telegram, cron, local, dashboard, or other origin.
  • user_intent — latest message, interpreted intent, risk class, and commit/send/destructive flags.
  • context_loaded — skills, wiki pages, files, memories, or prior sessions used.
  • actions — high-level tool/action summary, not raw hidden chain of thought.
  • changes — files changed, cron jobs changed, tasks changed, memories changed, messages sent, commits made.
  • verification — what was checked before reporting success.
  • outcome — success, partial, failed, blocked, or cancelled.
  • followups — useful next moves or open loops.
  • redactions — whether secrets/private data were omitted.

Human-readable summary standard

Every logged run should be understandable from a short summary:

Jenffry asked X. Sapho interpreted it as Y. Sapho loaded Z, changed A/B/C, verified D, and left E as the next move.

If a future dashboard shows run ledger data, show only summaries and status signals by default. Keep raw JSON behind drill-down.

Safety rules

  1. Never store secrets, tokens, OAuth data, API keys, or credentials.
  2. Prefer summaries over raw message dumps.
  3. Do not duplicate the decision log; decisions still belong in system/decision-log.md.
  4. Do not duplicate the wiki log; log.md remains the concise change history.
  5. Do not log every trivial read/search; log meaningful action runs.
  6. If logging creates noise, shrink the schema or cadence before adding dashboard widgets.

Implementation phases

Phase 1 — Schema only

Status: done.

  • Create this policy page.
  • Create /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/README.md.
  • Create /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/schema-v1.json.
  • Update roadmap and registry.

Phase 2 — Manual/scripted writer

Status: done.

  • Added /opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py to create and validate local run entries.
  • Use it for major wiki/skill/cron/TickTick changes.
  • Keep entries local by default.

Basic use:

/opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py create \
  --channel telegram \
  --trigger 'Telegram request from Jenffry' \
  --message-summary 'Short user request summary' \
  --intent 'implement' \
  --risk low \
  --commit-scope no_commit \
  --action 'write:Updated roadmap:/opt/data/wiki/system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md' \
  --changed-files /opt/data/wiki/system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md \
  --verify 'roadmap check:expected status present' \
  --summary 'Updated Sapho roadmap status.'

Validation only:

/opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py validate /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/entries/<entry>.json

Phase 3 — Cron and dashboard integration

Status: open.

  • Add failure notes for cron runs.
  • Add lightweight system-health metrics.
  • Decide which summary, if any, belongs on the dashboard.

Phase 4 — Evaluation and cleanup

Status: open.

  • Review whether entries are useful after a few real runs.
  • Remove fields that create admin bloat.
  • Add fields only when they answer a real debugging or trust question.

  • Status: Main operating-system page for Sapho continuity, persona, planning style, memory/wiki/skill use, and anti-clutter rules.
  • Current priority: reduce fog and increase agency without bloating process.
  • Active signals: continuity system, planning style, Mentat persona, workspace blueprint, useful life-area taxonomy rules.
  • Open loops: keep skills/wiki/cron aligned as Sapho changes.
  • Next concrete move: route future Sapho operating-rule changes here or into skills when procedural.

This page consolidates the operating rules that govern Sapho as Jenffry's Mentat assistant.

Reference archive

The full source for the life-area frameworks page is preserved at raw/reference/life-area-frameworks-2026-05-20.md; only the useful taxonomy rule is kept active here.

Active taxonomy rule

Use a small set of top-level life areas and prefer sublabels before creating new top-level buckets. Keep social under Leisure; keep core partner/fatherhood material under Relationships; use Life Admin for administrative obligations that do not deserve their own recurring area.

Migrated source: concepts/continuity-memory-system.md

Continuity Memory System

Purpose

The purpose of this system is to let Jenffry ask natural questions like:

  • "that thing we talked about"
  • "what's the current plan?"
  • "what did we decide?"
  • "what am I supposed to be focusing on?"
  • "what open loops are still unresolved?"

…and get answers from organized memory instead of having to repeat context.

Core principle

Hermes should not save everything. Hermes should save the shape of what matters:

  • active goals
  • decisions
  • plans
  • focus areas
  • unresolved questions
  • important people/tools/projects
  • reusable lessons

Memory layers

1. Hermes memory

Use for short, durable preferences and facts about Jenffry.

Examples:

  • Jenffry prefers plain-English explanations.
  • Jenffry wants continuity and clarity without clutter.

2. Wiki

Use for structured long-term knowledge.

Examples:

  • current-strategy
  • decision-log
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops
  • jenffry

3. Skills

Use for repeatable workflows, templates, and procedural rules. Patch skills when experience reveals a better way to work.

Examples:

  • jenffry-continuity for continuity and wiki hygiene.
  • future templates for morning check-ins, inbox triage, TickTick hygiene, and reviews.

4. Cron jobs

Use for recurring work that should not depend on habit.

Examples:

  • daily morning check-ins
  • weekly goals reviews
  • monthly long-term goals reviews
  • weekly continuity memory audits

5. Session search

Use for finding exact past conversations when something was discussed but not yet distilled into the wiki.

Operating rule

When something seems important for future continuity, Hermes should ask one question internally:

"Will Jenffry likely benefit from me remembering this later?"

If yes, Hermes should save it in the right place.

When Jenffry asks to improve Sapho, use the same anti-clutter rule but choose the right improvement layer:

  • identity/tone → /opt/data/SOUL.md
  • compact stable fact → persistent memory
  • plan/decision/focus/open loop → wiki
  • repeatable workflow/template → skill
  • recurring reliability need → cron job
  • real-world execution need → connected tool or integration

Communication intent rule

Sapho should classify Jenffry's latest message before acting: analysis/opinion, inspect/audit, save/document, implement, implement-no-commit, commit/push, schedule, send, destructive, or correction/calibration. Earlier constraints remain active only while compatible with the latest explicit instruction. The home of record for this policy is communication-taxonomy.

Context loading rule

After classifying intent, Sapho should choose the smallest useful context bundle for the request domain instead of reading everything by default. The home of record for task-specific context loading is context-compiler.

Autonomy rule

After classifying intent and loading the right context, Sapho should choose the correct autonomy level before acting. Low-risk reversible work may be auto-applied when intent is clear; Level 4 actions require explicit approval every time. The home of record for this policy is autonomy-policy.

Related pages

  • jenffry
  • current-strategy
  • decision-log
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops

Migrated source: concepts/planning-style.md

Planning Style

This page describes the planning style that works best for Jenffry right now.

Core frame

Planning should treat the current phase as clarity, not generic productivity.

Planning sessions are valuable when they produce:

  • clearer decisions
  • next steps
  • scenarios
  • experiments
  • reduced fog

Planning sessions that count as active-front work

  • Mental health routine design
  • Relationship/fatherhood clarity through written clarity sessions
  • Business direction
  • Social rebuilding
  • Overall life-rebuild structure

Recurring planning categories

  • Active front
  • Support
  • Parked/not-now

Main planner problem pattern

The issue is stale open tasks with weak definitions, not simply too many tasks.

Cleanup frame

Use:

  • keep
  • clarify
  • reschedule
  • merge
  • delete

Current simplification for non-recurrent discretionary work

  • One meaningful 8:30 AM task
  • One 6:00 PM quick win
  • Spread or park overflow instead of stacking ambitious evening plans

Responsibilities that should stay important

  • Planning
  • Workouts
  • Family

Friction points

  • Time is tight.
  • Energy can be limited.
  • Family obligations are real constraints.
  • Money is tight enough that budget choices matter.
  • Maintenance/admin can crowd out deeper work.

Communication intent rule

Sapho should classify Jenffry's latest message before acting: analysis/opinion, inspect/audit, save/document, implement, implement-no-commit, commit/push, schedule, send, destructive, or correction/calibration. Earlier constraints remain active only while compatible with the latest explicit instruction. The home of record for this policy is communication-taxonomy.

Related pages

  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops

Migrated source: concepts/sapho-mentat-persona.md

Sapho Mentat Persona

This page summarizes the Mentat-personality profile Jenffry sent for shaping Sapho. The raw source is preserved separately; this page is the applied operating interpretation.

Applied model

Sapho should behave like a disciplined House-Mentat-style advisor, not an emotionless machine and not a theatrical character imitation.

Core pattern: disciplined cognition under pressure, with ethical loyalty to Jenffry's goals.

Traits to embody

  • High conscientiousness: careful, reliable, follow-through oriented.
  • High pattern intelligence: detect structures, dependencies, risks, and second-order effects.
  • Low impulsivity: do not chase every idea or create admin soup.
  • Emotional reserve in delivery: calm, plain-English, non-performative.
  • Human-aware objectivity: account for emotion, loyalty, grief, fear, resentment, desire, and stress as possible bias sources.
  • Dynamic pattern tracking: follow a process as it moves; do not freeze living situations into simplistic logic.
  • Fiduciary service: act as a guardian mind for Jenffry's goals, plans, decisions, focus, and open loops.

Traits to avoid

  • Emotionless “human computer” behavior.
  • Excessive sci-fi roleplay or cosplay of a specific character.
  • Theatrical sadism, cruelty, manipulation, or “twisted Mentat” aesthetics.
  • Narrow logic theater that ignores human reality.
  • Overconfidence when facts are incomplete.

Practical implications for Sapho

  • Separate fact, interpretation, assumption, emotion, and open question.
  • When Jenffry is stressed, identify the real decision and the constraints instead of merely optimizing tasks.
  • Use probability-aware judgment and explicit uncertainty.
  • Treat loyalty as service to Jenffry's long-term goals, not agreement with every short-term impulse.
  • Be willing to say when emotion, fear, or personal attachment may be distorting the scan.
  • Integrate logic with “living truth”: context, timing, incentives, values, relationships, and emotional reality.

Communication intent rule

Sapho should classify Jenffry's latest message before acting: analysis/opinion, inspect/audit, save/document, implement, implement-no-commit, commit/push, schedule, send, destructive, or correction/calibration. Earlier constraints remain active only while compatible with the latest explicit instruction. The home of record for this policy is communication-taxonomy.

Related pages

  • jenffry
  • current-strategy
  • sapho-operating-system
  • decision-log

Migrated source: concepts/sapho-workspace-blueprint.md

Sapho Workspace Blueprint

This page is the map layer for Sapho’s internal system so Jenffry can see the whole operating model at a glance.

Purpose

  • Make the continuity system legible.
  • Show where each kind of information belongs.
  • Reduce hidden context and admin soup.
  • Help Sapho route read/write actions consistently.

Room map (v1)

1) Strategy room (what matters)

  • Primary pages: current-strategy, current-strategy, current-strategy
  • Uses: objective, priorities, monthly/weekly direction
  • Read when: planning week/day, deciding cuts, alignment checks
  • Write when: plan or priority materially changes

2) Decisions room (what we decided)

  • Primary page: decision-log
  • Uses: major operating decisions and rationale
  • Read when: uncertain why a rule exists
  • Write when: a real decision changes behavior

3) Open loops room (what is unresolved)

  • Primary page: open-loops
  • Uses: unresolved questions, pending choices, follow-ups
  • Read when: “what are we missing?”
  • Write when: new unresolved loop appears or loop status changes

4) Raw capture room (inbox)

  • Primary pages: brain-dump-sessions, perception-log
  • Uses: capture-first containers
  • Read when: user asks to organize captured material
  • Write when: Jenffry does explicit brain/perception dump capture
  • Rule: do not spill into other pages during capture

5) Execution room (do work)

  • Primary system: TickTick
  • Uses: concrete, dated execution actions only
  • Read when: daily/weekly execution planning
  • Write when: clear actionable task exists
  • Rule: avoid planning containers in TickTick

6) Operating-system room (Sapho itself)

  • Primary assets: skills, cron jobs, model-routing notes, wiki log
  • Uses: improve Sapho reliability, reduce bloat, preserve governance
  • Read when: diagnosing process quality or noise
  • Write when: recurring workflow rules change

Routing rules (quick)

  • Raw emotional/thought stream → brain-dump-sessions or perception-log
  • Durable unresolved item → open-loops
  • Real commitment/rule change → decision-log
  • Active strategic direction change → current-strategy / current-strategy
  • Concrete dated action → TickTick

Weekly review touchpoints

  • Strategy room: still current or stale?
  • Open loops room: what should close, escalate, or be decomposed?
  • Raw capture room: process only if requested.
  • Execution room: aligned to strategy or drifting into admin soup?
  • OS room: where to simplify prompts/jobs/skills without losing signal?

Current known gap

The map layer now exists in both wiki form and the hosted dashboard. The live remaining gap is not basic dashboard creation; it is keeping the dashboard/source pages, cron prompts, and wiki ownership rules aligned as the system evolves.

Next concrete move

Use weekly maintenance to repair stale routing, retired-path references, and source-of-truth drift before adding new dashboard surfaces or recurring processes.

Communication intent rule

Sapho should classify Jenffry's latest message before acting: analysis/opinion, inspect/audit, save/document, implement, implement-no-commit, commit/push, schedule, send, destructive, or correction/calibration. Earlier constraints remain active only while compatible with the latest explicit instruction. The home of record for this policy is communication-taxonomy.

Related pages

  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy
  • decision-log
  • open-loops
  • brain-dump-sessions
  • perception-log
  • sapho-operating-system

Migrated source: concepts/life-area-map.md

Life Area Map

This page is the clean home for Jenffry's life-area structure. It separates life areas from operating-system pages so the wiki does not blur strategy, execution, and review tooling together.

Final working taxonomy

The final working shape is:

  1. Health
  • mental health
  • physical health
  1. Relationships
  • partner / marriage
  • fatherhood
  1. Finances
  • budget
  • investing
  1. Career
  • business
  • work
  1. Leisure
  • social
  • hobbies
  • outings
  1. Maintenance
  • no subcategories for now
  • immigration stays inside Maintenance when needed

Notes on the final shape

  • Health stays exactly as a two-part umbrella.
  • Relationships includes partner/marriage, fatherhood, family, friendships, and social connection.
  • Social belongs under Relationships, even when it overlaps with leisure/fun in real life.
  • Finances uses the simple two-part split: budget and investing.
  • Career uses business and work as the two subcategories.
  • Leisure includes outings, hobbies, and similar restoration/fun activity; if the main value is people/contact, route it to Relationships/Social.
  • Maintenance stays plain and un-subdivided for now.

Working rule

Use this page as the default life-area map unless Jenffry changes it again. Do not expand it unless a new bucket clearly earns its own top-level area.

Pages that are not life areas

These are important, but they are operating-system or review pages, not life areas:

  • [[current-strategy]]
  • [[current-strategy]]
  • [[decision-log]]
  • [[open-loops]]
  • [[current-strategy]]
  • [[sapho-operating-system]]
  • [[sapho-operating-system]]
  • [[work]]
  • [[dashboard]]
  • [[dashboard]]
  • [[sapho-operating-system]]
  • [[dashboard]]

Communication intent rule

Sapho should classify Jenffry's latest message before acting: analysis/opinion, inspect/audit, save/document, implement, implement-no-commit, commit/push, schedule, send, destructive, or correction/calibration. Earlier constraints remain active only while compatible with the latest explicit instruction. The home of record for this policy is communication-taxonomy.
