Archived source pointer

Full source preserved: raw/reference/life-area-frameworks-2026-05-20.md.

Standing automation: Sapho system improvement maintenance

The weekly cron job a42c23ac4083 runs as Weekly Sapho system improvement maintenance. Its role is to keep the whole Sapho operating system healthy, not just collect missed memory. It should route durable session signal into the current life-area/system/inbox structure, repair stale links after migrations, keep index.md and dashboard summaries aligned, audit cron/skill/dashboard/source-of-truth drift, and avoid creating new process unless it clearly reduces future confusion. Delivery is local-only by default.

Upgrade roadmap

Future Sapho system upgrades are tracked in sapho-upgrade-roadmap. Use that page as the parking and prioritization layer for Sapho 2.0 ideas before promoting them into concrete plans, skill patches, cron changes, evals, or code/tooling work. The source document for the current roadmap is preserved at raw/reference/sapho-system-improvement-plan-2026-05-20.md.

Sapho System Map
system/sapho-system-map.md
degree 2

Status: One-page architecture map for Sapho as Jenffry's personal Mentat operating system. Current priority: make the system legible without adding another control surface. Active signals: identity, memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, raw evidence, backups,

No resolved wikilinks.

Sapho System Map

Dashboard summary

  • Status: One-page architecture map for Sapho as Jenffry's personal Mentat operating system.
  • Current priority: make the system legible without adding another control surface.
  • Active signals: identity, memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, raw evidence, backups, logs, evals, dashboard, and guardrails.
  • Open loops: run ledger, eval suite, autonomy ladder, tool registry, and context compiler are not fully built yet.
  • Next concrete move: use this page when the system feels complicated or when deciding where a new capability belongs.

One-line model

Sapho is Jenffry's personal Mentat system: a layered assistant designed to reduce fog, increase agency, and preserve continuity without becoming admin soup.

Layers

Identity

  • Home: /opt/data/SOUL.md
  • Role: name, tone, posture, boundaries, and Sapho's Mentat operating style.
  • Rule: stable identity lives here, not scattered through cron prompts.

Memory

  • Home: persistent memory.
  • Role: tiny durable facts that should affect every future session.
  • Rule: memory is not a mini-wiki. It stays compact.

Wiki

  • Home: /opt/data/wiki
  • Role: structured continuity: plans, life areas, decisions, open loops, dashboard summaries, raw/source material.
  • Current spine:
  • areas/ — Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, Life Admin.
  • system/ — strategy, decisions, open loops, Sapho operating rules, dashboard, registries.
  • inbox/ — active raw capture.
  • raw/ — evidence and reference.
  • plans/ — migration/implementation plans.

Skills

  • Home: /opt/data/skills
  • Role: repeatable procedures.
  • Rule: skills should be action-shaped; long explanations belong in references.

Cron

  • Home: scheduler jobs plus future system/cron-registry.md if needed.
  • Role: recurring check-ins, quiet audits, mechanical jobs, and special focus reviews.
  • Rule: cron should reduce reliance on habit, not create review fatigue.

TickTick

  • Home: TickTick.
  • Role: concrete execution tasks only.
  • Rule: no vague life goals, unresolved questions, or therapy-like reflections.

Raw evidence

  • Home: raw/ and active inbox/ pages.
  • Role: source material, transcripts, exports, and reference documents.
  • Rule: preserve raw first, synthesize second.

Backups

  • Home: /opt/data/backups/ and wiki backup folders.
  • Role: recovery and history.
  • Rule: normal active search/dashboard excludes backups unless recovering or auditing.

Logs / run ledger

  • Current home: system logs and wiki log.md.
  • Future home: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/.
  • Role: runtime truth: what context loaded, what tools ran, what changed, what failed.
  • Status: open upgrade.

Evals

  • Future home: /opt/data/evals/sapho/.
  • Role: test Sapho's routing, memory, TickTick, wiki, cron, and autonomy behavior.
  • Status: open upgrade.

Safety / guardrails

  • Home: future system/autonomy-policy.md and system/tool-registry.md, or consolidated into system/sapho-operating-system.md until the pages earn their keep.
  • Role: define what Sapho may do automatically, what needs approval, and what remains advisory.
  • Status: open upgrade.

Dashboard

  • Home: system/dashboard.md plus hosted GitHub Pages dashboard.
  • Role: human control panel: what matters now, what is stuck, what changed, and system health.
  • Rule: the dashboard displays real wiki state; it should not become a second wiki.

Sapho 2.0 thesis

Sapho's next phase is not more structure for its own sake. It is:

  • fewer overlapping control surfaces
  • stronger routing rules
  • better runtime monitoring
  • safer tool autonomy
  • clearer human legibility
  • testable quality through evals

Highest-leverage upgrades

  1. Source-of-truth registry — done as system/source-of-truth-registry.md.
  2. Context compiler — open.
  3. Run ledger / flight recorder — open.
  4. Eval suite — open.
  5. Autonomy ladder / tool registry — open.

Source

This map was created from the uploaded Sapho System Improvement Plan, preserved at:

  • raw/reference/sapho-system-improvement-plan-2026-05-20.md
Sapho Upgrade Roadmap
system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md
degree 6

Status: Active roadmap for future Sapho system upgrades. Current priority: improve routing discipline, observability, safety, and human legibility without adding bloat. Active signals: source-of-truth registry now includes canonical object ownership rules, com

No resolved wikilinks.

Sapho Upgrade Roadmap

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active roadmap for future Sapho system upgrades.
  • Current priority: improve routing discipline, observability, safety, and human legibility without adding bloat.
  • Active signals: source-of-truth registry now includes canonical object ownership rules, communication taxonomy now clarifies analysis/implementation/commit intent, context compiler Phase 1 now defines task-specific context bundles, autonomy policy now defines Level 0–4 approval boundaries, initial communication-intent and autonomy-level eval examples exist, and the run ledger schema plus lightweight writer exist under /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/ and /opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py.
  • Open loops: page metadata normalization, context compiler testing/automation decision, run ledger adoption/automation, routing/memory/TickTick/wiki/cron evals, cron registry, prompt linting, and dashboard control-panel refinement.
  • Next concrete move: choose the next roadmap item only when it reduces confusion, improves safety/trust, or removes more process than it adds.

Purpose

This page stores future Sapho upgrades so they do not scatter across open loops, skills, cron prompts, memory, and chat history.

The rule for this page:

Sapho upgrade ideas live here until they become a concrete implementation plan, decision, skill patch, cron change, or code/tooling project.

Source document

The raw uploaded plan is preserved at:

  • raw/reference/sapho-system-improvement-plan-2026-05-20.md

This roadmap is the synthesized working version. The raw file remains the evidence/source version.

Core diagnosis

Sapho is already a serious layered operating system: identity/persona, persistent memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, raw capture, backups, dashboard, and external tools.

The next phase should not be “add more stuff.” The next phase is:

Sapho 2.0 = same layered system, but with fewer overlapping control surfaces, stronger routing rules, better runtime monitoring, safer tool autonomy, and clearer human legibility.

Current risks:

  • Planning overlap and duplicated control surfaces.
  • Skills becoming manuals instead of action-shaped procedures.
  • Cron review bloat and possible review fatigue.
  • TickTick pollution from vague planning residue.
  • Memory duplication if memory mirrors wiki/skills/current state.
  • Prompt fragility in scheduled jobs.
  • Limited observability into meaningful agent runs.

Backbone upgrades

1. Source-of-truth registry

  • Status: started.
  • Home: system/source-of-truth-registry.md.
  • Purpose: define where each kind of information belongs.
  • Why it matters: prevents memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, and logs from all becoming valid-looking homes for the same thing.

2. Context compiler

  • Status: Phase 1 policy/template created.
  • Home: system/context-compiler.md.
  • Purpose: load only the right context for the current task.
  • First version: policy/template, not full automation.
  • Examples:
  • finance request → finance area + current strategy + finance skill
  • relationship request → relationships area + relevant open loops + boundaries
  • system request → Sapho OS + registry + Hermes docs
  • execution request → TickTick rules + current strategy

3. Run ledger / flight recorder

  • Status: schema and lightweight writer created.
  • Home: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/ for runtime entries; system/run-ledger.md for human-readable policy; /opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py for manual/scripted entry creation.
  • Purpose: trace meaningful Sapho runs: context loaded, skills used, tools called, files changed, tasks created, decisions made, open loops updated, errors, approval requirements, final summary.
  • Why it matters: debugging, rollback, trust, bloat detection, and model comparison.

4. Autonomy and safety policy

  • Status: Phase 1 policy created.
  • Home: system/autonomy-policy.md.
  • Purpose: define what Sapho may do automatically, what requires approval, and what remains advisory only.
  • Core ladder:
  • Level 0 — advisory only
  • Level 1 — draft only
  • Level 2 — apply with approval
  • Level 3 — auto-apply low-risk reversible actions
  • Level 4 — explicit approval every time for destructive, external, money, credential/privacy, legal/medical/immigration, broad automation, or high-blast-radius changes

5. Eval suite

  • Status: initial communication-intent and autonomy-level examples started.
  • Home: /opt/data/evals/sapho/.
  • Purpose: test Sapho’s routing, memory, TickTick, wiki, cron, and safety behavior with golden examples.
  • Current suites: communication-intent-v1.jsonl, autonomy-levels-v1.jsonl.
  • Next categories: routing, memory, TickTick, wiki, cron.

Staged implementation order

Stage 1 — Stabilize architecture

  • [x] Preserve raw Sapho improvement plan.
  • [x] Create source-of-truth registry.
  • [x] Create one-page Sapho system map.
  • [x] Create this upgrade roadmap.
  • [~] Canonical object ownership rules added to system/source-of-truth-registry.md; full page metadata normalization remains open.
  • [ ] Restructure system/open-loops.md into active, parked, needs decision, needs information, system, and retired sections.

Stage 1.5 — Canonical homes / source-of-truth ownership

  • [x] Add concise canonical object ownership rules for major Sapho surfaces.
  • [x] Define the rule: skills own method, wiki owns truth, cron owns recurrence, TickTick owns concrete execution, dashboard displays, raw preserves evidence, backups preserve recovery history.
  • [ ] Use the weekly Sapho system improvement maintenance job to detect and repair ownership drift.
  • [ ] Add page-level metadata only where it prevents real routing confusion; do not tag every tiny file.

Stage 1.6 — Communication taxonomy / command intent

  • [x] Create system/communication-taxonomy.md as the home of record for interpreting Jenffry's command language and active constraints.
  • [x] Define intent categories: analysis/opinion, inspect/audit, save/document, implement, implement-no-commit, commit/push, schedule, send, destructive, and correction/calibration.
  • [x] Define constraint inheritance: latest explicit instruction wins; earlier constraints persist only while compatible.
  • [x] Add initial eval examples for ambiguous command handling and commit/no-commit supersession at /opt/data/evals/sapho/communication-intent-v1.jsonl.

Stage 1.7 — Context compiler / task-specific context loading

  • [x] Create system/context-compiler.md as the Phase 1 policy/template for task-specific context loading.
  • [x] Define domain bundles for Sapho system, TickTick/execution, current strategy, finance, health, relationships, career, leisure, life admin, raw capture, and external research.
  • [ ] Test the policy against real requests before deciding whether automation is worth it.

Stage 1.8 — Autonomy policy / Level 4 boundaries

  • [x] Create system/autonomy-policy.md as the home of record for Sapho action-permission levels.
  • [x] Define Levels 0–4, with Level 4 requiring explicit approval every time for destructive, external, money, credential/privacy, legal/medical/immigration, broad automation, or high-blast-radius changes.
  • [x] Add eval examples for Level 4 boundaries at /opt/data/evals/sapho/autonomy-levels-v1.jsonl.
  • [ ] Test the policy during real work before adding automation.

Stage 2 — Reduce bloat

  • [ ] Compress system/current-strategy.md so it stays scannable.
  • [ ] Keep broad goals cascade as strategy machinery, not duplicated life-area content.
  • [ ] Audit jenffry-continuity for manual bloat and move long explanations into references only if the skill becomes unwieldy in practice.
  • [ ] Audit ticktick-task-management for action-firewall clarity.
  • [ ] Audit cron prompts for duplicate reflection questions.

Stage 3 — Make cron safer and more legible

  • [ ] Create system/cron-registry.md only if cron complexity remains hard to reason about.
  • [ ] Categorize jobs as anchor, quiet governance, mechanical, or special focus.
  • [ ] Identify overlapping reflection jobs.
  • [ ] Add prompt linting/sanitization for cron prompts if feasible.
  • [ ] Use shadow mode for new automations when the output/action risk is non-trivial.

Stage 4 — Protect execution

  • [ ] Strengthen TickTick action firewall.
  • [ ] Weekly hygiene should classify stale tasks as do, reschedule, clarify, move to wiki, or archive/delete.
  • [ ] Move vague tasks back to wiki/open loops instead of keeping them in TickTick.
  • [ ] Define or reinforce task naming standard.

Stage 5 — Add observability

  • [x] Design run ledger schema.
  • [x] Add a lightweight run ledger writer for meaningful tool/file/task/decision changes.
  • [ ] Use the writer on several real runs, then decide whether automatic logging is worth it.
  • [ ] Add failure notes for cron runs.
  • [ ] Add lightweight system health metrics.
  • [ ] Decide what belongs on dashboard system-health view.

Stage 6 — Add evals and guardrails

  • [ ] Create first 20 Sapho evals.
  • [ ] Add routing evals.
  • [ ] Add TickTick evals.
  • [ ] Add memory evals.
  • [ ] Add wiki/source-of-truth evals.
  • [ ] Add autonomy levels and approval rules.
  • [ ] Add tool registry if tool risk/complexity justifies a standalone page.

Parking lot / future upgrades

  • Context compiler.
  • Open-loop aging system.
  • Duplicate detector across wiki, skills, cron prompts, and memory.
  • Sapho diff mode for important edits.
  • Personal operating metrics.
  • Skill test cases.
  • Model-routing policy refinements.
  • Evidence-linked wiki updates.
  • Sensitive-domain boundaries.
  • Shadow mode for new automations.
  • Backup search exclusion policy.
  • Dashboard as control panel rather than second wiki.
  • Voice-output policy for long answers.

Implementation restraint

Do not implement every item here just because it exists. This roadmap is a parking and prioritization layer. Each upgrade should pass the test:

  • Does it reduce future confusion?
  • Does it improve safety, trust, or observability?
  • Does it prevent duplication?
  • Does it avoid adding more process than it removes?

If not, leave it parked.

Source of Truth Registry
system/source-of-truth-registry.md
degree 2

Status: Active routing constitution for Sapho's information layers. Current priority: prevent duplication across memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, dashboard summaries, raw files, and logs. Active signals: Sapho 2.0 should compress control surfaces, strengt

No resolved wikilinks.

Source of Truth Registry

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active routing constitution for Sapho's information layers.
  • Current priority: prevent duplication across memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, dashboard summaries, raw files, and logs.
  • Active signals: Sapho 2.0 should compress control surfaces, strengthen routing, define canonical object ownership, use the context compiler to load the right task-specific context, define autonomy/Level 4 approval boundaries, and improve observability.
  • Open loops: context compiler testing, autonomy evals, full eval suite, and tool registry remain future upgrades.
  • Next concrete move: use this registry before adding or editing major pages, memories, skills, cron jobs, dashboard state, raw files, or TickTick tasks.

Purpose

This registry defines the canonical home for each kind of information in Sapho. The rule is simple:

Before saving or changing anything, ask: what is the home of record for this?

The goal is not to store less useful context. The goal is to stop the same context from living in five places where it slowly diverges.

Routing table

Identity, tone, and stable behavior

  • Canonical home: /opt/data/SOUL.md
  • Also allowed: compact persistent memory for high-value stable preferences; system/sapho-operating-system.md for structured explanation.
  • Not allowed: TickTick, random cron prompts, duplicated skill manuals.

Stable user preferences

  • Canonical home: persistent memory.
  • Also allowed: wiki only when the preference needs structured context or history.
  • Not allowed: current plan pages, TickTick, raw logs as the only source.

Durable plans and life context

  • Canonical home: /opt/data/wiki.
  • Current structure:
  • system/current-strategy.md for active strategy/focus/goals cascade.
  • areas/ for life-area context.
  • system/open-loops.md for unresolved questions.
  • system/decision-log.md for decisions.
  • Not allowed: persistent memory unless it is a tiny stable fact; TickTick unless it has a concrete next action.

Active priorities and focus hierarchy

  • Canonical home: system/current-strategy.md.
  • Not allowed: duplicated independent focus pages or cron prompts as the only source.

Decisions

  • Canonical home: system/decision-log.md.
  • Format: lightweight decision record: date, status, context, decision, rationale, consequences, links.
  • Not allowed: memory unless the decision creates a stable preference; TickTick.

Open unresolved questions

  • Canonical home: system/open-loops.md.
  • Rule: an open loop must eventually be promoted, parked, resolved, retired, or converted into a concrete next action.
  • Not allowed: TickTick unless the next action is visible and executable.

Repeatable procedures

  • Canonical home: skills.
  • Rule: skill body should be action-shaped; long explanation belongs in skill references.
  • Not allowed: wiki planning pages as the only procedural source.

Scheduled rhythms and recurring automation

  • Canonical home: cron jobs plus system/cron-registry.md if/when created.
  • Also allowed: system/sapho-operating-system.md for policy; system/decision-log.md for decisions.
  • Not allowed: TickTick unless there is a user-facing action.

Concrete execution tasks

  • Canonical home: TickTick.
  • Admission rule: a task needs a verb, object, clear done condition, life area, and a date only if timing matters.
  • Not allowed: wiki, memory, or open loops as a substitute for executable tasks.

Raw dumps and evidence

  • Canonical home: raw/ for source material, inbox/ for active capture.
  • Examples: inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md, inbox/perception-log.md, raw/transcripts/, raw/reference/.
  • Not allowed: main planning pages without synthesis.

Historical versions and backups

  • Canonical home: backups/ or /opt/data/backups/.
  • Restore helper: /opt/data/scripts/sapho_restore.py lists backups, stages selected paths, restores selected paths with pre-restore safety copies, and can dry-run Git restores in the safe repo.
  • Backup job: cron job ff09fc1ab416 runs /opt/data/scripts/sapho_system_backup.sh daily at 08:00 UTC; it captures live uncommitted wiki/skills/scripts/cron/evals into the safe copy and local archive, and captures logs/agent-runs in the local full restore archive.
  • Rule: normal active search excludes backups; recovery/audit search may include them.
  • Not allowed: active dashboard/search scope unless explicitly requested.

Runtime traces / flight recorder

  • Canonical home: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/ for runtime entries and schema.
  • Human policy page: system/run-ledger.md.
  • Current files: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/schema-v1.json, /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/README.md, future /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/entries/ JSON entries, and writer script /opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py.
  • Rule: log meaningful action runs only; do not store secrets, raw transcripts, or every trivial lookup.
  • Not allowed: decision log as a dumping ground for every tool call.
  • Status: schema and lightweight writer created; automatic logging not yet enabled.

Test cases and evals

  • Canonical home: /opt/data/evals/sapho/.
  • Current suite: communication-intent-v1.jsonl seeds ambiguous command handling, commit/no-commit supersession, side-effect, destructive-action, and correction/calibration tests; autonomy-levels-v1.jsonl seeds Level 0–4 autonomy tests with emphasis on Level 4 boundaries and Level 3 backup follow-through.
  • Not allowed: long test banks inside skill bodies.
  • Status: initial communication-intent and autonomy-level examples started; full automated eval runner remains a future upgrade.

Dashboard-facing state

  • Canonical home: ## Dashboard summary sections inside real wiki pages.
  • Not allowed: a duplicate dashboard-only mini-wiki.

Canonical object ownership rules

The routing table above defines the home for each kind of information. This section defines the ownership rule for the major Sapho objects that can otherwise drift into duplicate sources of truth.

Use this format mentally before editing: object → job → owns → does not own → source-of-truth rule.

Core identity and always-loaded context

/opt/data/SOUL.md

  • Job: define Sapho's stable identity, tone, role, and user-facing operating posture.
  • Owns: Sapho persona, name/role, default communication style, broad behavioral boundaries.
  • Does not own: detailed procedures, live plans, open loops, cron behavior, or project state.
  • Source-of-truth rule: if tone/persona guidance conflicts with a skill or wiki note, SOUL.md wins for user-facing behavior; skills may only add task-specific procedure.

Persistent memory / user profile

  • Job: keep compact facts that should affect every session.
  • Owns: durable user preferences, stable environment facts, recurring corrections.
  • Does not own: current tasks, project state, full procedures, open loops, raw emotional state, or detailed decisions.
  • Source-of-truth rule: memory should point Sapho in the right direction quickly; structured content belongs in the wiki and procedures belong in skills.

Core wiki system pages

system/current-strategy.md

  • Job: current strategic control tower.
  • Owns: active fronts, current priorities, near-term direction, waiting/blocked items, and this week's operating rule.
  • Does not own: raw dumps, full life-area histories, detailed procedures, or executable task lists.
  • Source-of-truth rule: this page wins for what matters now; area pages win for domain context and TickTick wins for concrete dated actions.

system/open-loops.md

  • Job: unresolved questions and pending choices.
  • Owns: active, parked, decision-needed, information-needed, system, monitor-only, and retired loops.
  • Does not own: executable tasks, broad raw dumps, resolved decisions, or live strategy summaries.
  • Source-of-truth rule: if a loop has a clear next action, convert it to TickTick or a plan; if it becomes a decision, move it to system/decision-log.md; otherwise keep it here or park/retire it.

system/decision-log.md

  • Job: record decisions that explain current behavior.
  • Owns: accepted/replaced/retired decisions, rationale, consequences, and links.
  • Does not own: vague notes, open questions, raw transcripts, or every small implementation step.
  • Source-of-truth rule: if a rule affects future Sapho behavior or Jenffry's operating system, the decision belongs here; implementation details may live in skills, cron prompts, or code.

system/sapho-operating-system.md

  • Job: human-readable operating doctrine for Sapho as an assistant system.
  • Owns: high-level principles, layer boundaries, anti-clutter doctrine, and Sapho system philosophy.
  • Does not own: detailed how-to procedures, live strategy, raw source documents, or every cron prompt.
  • Source-of-truth rule: this page explains why the system works this way; source-of-truth-registry.md owns routing rules and skills own procedures.

system/communication-taxonomy.md

  • Job: command-language and active-intent policy.
  • Owns: how Sapho interprets analysis vs audit vs save vs implement vs commit vs send vs destructive instructions, and how later instructions supersede earlier constraints.
  • Does not own: general information routing, tool safety details, or the actual work product.
  • Source-of-truth rule: when user intent or commit/no-commit scope is ambiguous, classify the latest message using this page before acting.

system/context-compiler.md

  • Job: task-specific context loading policy.
  • Owns: which pages, skills, tools, and boundaries Sapho should load for each request domain.
  • Does not own: the source content itself, canonical write-routing, or implemented automation.
  • Source-of-truth rule: after intent classification and before substantial work, use this page to choose the smallest useful context bundle.

system/autonomy-policy.md

  • Job: action-permission and approval-boundary policy.
  • Owns: Sapho autonomy levels, Level 4 boundaries, and when approval is required before acting.
  • Does not own: user intent classification, context loading, canonical write-routing, or specific domain advice.
  • Source-of-truth rule: after intent classification and context loading, use this page to decide whether Sapho may act automatically or must ask for explicit approval.

system/source-of-truth-registry.md

  • Job: routing constitution.
  • Owns: canonical homes, object ownership rules, and write-routing discipline.
  • Does not own: the content being routed, detailed procedures, raw evidence, or runtime traces.
  • Source-of-truth rule: before creating another home for information, update or follow this registry.

system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md

  • Job: parking and prioritization layer for future Sapho upgrades.
  • Owns: proposed upgrades, staged improvement order, parking-lot ideas, and promotion criteria.
  • Does not own: implemented rules, live decisions, raw source plans, or current daily strategy.
  • Source-of-truth rule: future Sapho/Hermes/wiki/cron/skills/TickTick improvement ideas live here until promoted into a decision, skill patch, cron change, script, eval, or implementation plan.

system/run-ledger.md

  • Job: human-readable run ledger policy and schema explanation.
  • Owns: what should be logged, what should not be logged, safety rules, entry-shape explanation, and implementation phases.
  • Does not own: actual runtime entries, decisions, wiki change history, or raw transcripts.
  • Source-of-truth rule: runtime run entries and schema live under /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/; this wiki page explains the policy and keeps the design legible.

system/dashboard.md

  • Job: dashboard feed contract and implementation/governance notes.
  • Owns: how dashboard content is assembled, exclusions, and display rules.
  • Does not own: source-owned status widgets or duplicate life-area/system state.
  • Source-of-truth rule: dashboard-facing state belongs in the owning real page's ## Dashboard summary; the dashboard renders and links, it does not become a second wiki.

system/progress-tracking.md

  • Job: aggregate progress signals across daily/weekly/monthly review cadence.
  • Owns: compact progress trend summaries and tracking-signal interpretation.
  • Does not own: raw daily journals, TickTick task lists, or full life-area content.
  • Source-of-truth rule: review outputs may feed this page, but concrete tasks stay in TickTick and domain context stays in area pages.

Area, inbox, raw, and history layers

areas/ pages

  • Job: structured continuity for a life domain.
  • Owns: domain context, constraints, patterns, decisions linked to that area, and recurring review notes.
  • Does not own: global strategy, executable task lists, raw dumps before synthesis, or Sapho platform policy.
  • Source-of-truth rule: area pages own domain substance; current-strategy.md owns priority order and TickTick owns concrete execution.

inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md and inbox/perception-log.md

  • Job: active raw capture containers with light review/status.
  • Owns: captured raw entries, status markers, weekly local review summaries, and source-owned dashboard summaries.
  • Does not own: synthesized strategy, permanent decisions, or TickTick tasks unless Jenffry explicitly asks for processing.
  • Source-of-truth rule: capture first, synthesize later; do not spill raw material into planning pages during intake.

raw/

  • Job: preserve source material and evidence.
  • Owns: original documents, transcripts, source references, and pre-migration/pre-compression archives.
  • Does not own: current synthesized truth or live operating state.
  • Source-of-truth rule: raw files are evidence; live pages own the current interpretation.

/opt/data/backups/ and backup artifacts

  • Job: recovery history.
  • Owns: restore points and historical copies.
  • Does not own: live search results, active dashboard state, or current planning truth.
  • Source-of-truth rule: normal continuity work excludes backups unless doing recovery or audit.

Procedural and recurring-action layers

Skills

  • Job: reusable methods.
  • Owns: triggers, required inputs, procedure, output format, pitfalls, and verification steps.
  • Does not own: live user context, current strategy, raw evidence, or scheduled state.
  • Source-of-truth rule: skill owns how to do the work; wiki owns what is true; cron owns when it recurs.

Cron jobs

  • Job: scheduled execution or review rhythm.
  • Owns: cadence, delivery target, loaded skills, prompt, script, and expected output behavior.
  • Does not own: permanent policy, current strategy, decisions, raw evidence, or user tasks.
  • Source-of-truth rule: cron executes a rhythm; if its prompt contains durable policy, migrate that policy to the relevant skill/wiki page and have the prompt point there.

Weekly Sapho system improvement maintenance cron

  • Job: quiet weekly maintenance of Sapho's operating system.
  • Owns: detecting drift, applying small safe repairs, and flagging larger recommendations.
  • Does not own: permanent routing rules, decisions, or raw system history.
  • Source-of-truth rule: this job enforces the registry; it does not replace the registry.

Local scripts

  • Job: deterministic collection, publishing, backup, or verification.
  • Owns: repeatable mechanical behavior and script-specific diagnostics.
  • Does not own: system policy, decisions, or synthesized continuity.
  • Source-of-truth rule: scripts provide evidence or perform mechanical work; wiki/skills/cron define why and when they run.

Execution, display, and observability

TickTick

  • Job: execution layer.
  • Owns: concrete next actions, dated reminders, events, errands, follow-ups, and actionable checklist items.
  • Does not own: broad goals, unresolved life questions, raw dumps, Sapho upgrades, or strategy.
  • Source-of-truth rule: if it cannot be directly done or clearly completed, clarify it or route it to wiki/open loops/roadmap instead of TickTick.

Dashboard

  • Job: human control panel and live wiki renderer.
  • Owns: presentation, navigation, graph/display behavior, and surfaced summaries.
  • Does not own: original state that belongs in wiki pages, TickTick, cron, or logs.
  • Source-of-truth rule: dashboard displays; owning pages decide.

Future run ledger / flight recorder

  • Job: runtime truth for meaningful Sapho runs.
  • Owns: what context loaded, tools called, files changed, tasks created, errors, and final run summary.
  • Does not own: decisions, current strategy, raw user evidence, or procedures.
  • Source-of-truth rule: logs explain what happened; decision log explains what was decided.

Ownership enforcement rule

When a page, skill, cron job, dashboard section, or script starts storing content outside its job, do one of four things:

  1. Move the content to the canonical home.
  2. Replace the duplicate with a link to the canonical home.
  3. Preserve the old version in raw/ or backups if it has evidentiary value.
  4. Flag the conflict for Jenffry if the correct home is ambiguous or the change is high-impact.

Only define object ownership for surfaces that can cause routing confusion, duplication, or operational drift. Do not create metadata bureaucracy for tiny one-off files.

Routing discipline

When new information arrives:

  1. Identify the information type.
  2. Route it to the canonical home.
  3. If the canonical home changes, update this registry.
  4. If the information is temporary, avoid persistent memory.
  5. If the information is broad and unresolved, use open loops, not TickTick.
  6. If the information is executable, use TickTick, not the wiki.
  7. If the information is a procedure, use a skill.
  8. If the information is evidence, preserve it under raw/ before synthesis.

Source

This registry was created from the uploaded Sapho System Improvement Plan, preserved at:

  • raw/reference/sapho-system-improvement-plan-2026-05-20.md

Inbox

Brain Dump Sessions

Brain Dump Sessions

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active raw brain-dump inbox and quiet weekly review page.
  • Current priority: preserve raw thought streams here until Jenffry explicitly asks to organize/process them.
  • Active signals: one partially processed 2026-05-18 session; one new 2026-05-24 raw/unprocessed life-story session; weekly review summary pending first quiet dashboard-fed run.
  • Open loops: remaining raw items from the 2026-05-18 session are still pending organization.
  • Next concrete move: let the quiet review update status summaries here; only process/move raw material when Jenffry explicitly asks.

This page is the collection point for Jenffry's freeform brain dump sessions.

When Jenffry explicitly says "this is a brain dump session" or clearly identifies a message as a brain dump, capture what follows here first, without prematurely organizing, judging, or turning it into tasks.

Operating rule

  • Preserve the raw thought stream in this page under a dated session heading while it is still unprocessed or pending Jenffry approval.
  • Keep Jenffry's language and intent as intact as possible during capture.
  • Do not over-summarize during capture.
  • Do not add later-organization notes by default.
  • Do not move content into other wiki pages or TickTick during capture.
  • The brain dump page is the containment layer; processing happens later only if Jenffry explicitly asks to organize the dump.
  • Weekly review should run quietly in the background: inspect this page, update status/review summary sections in place, and make the output visible through the dashboard/wiki instead of sending a routine Telegram message.
  • Every active session must carry a visible processing status so weekly reviews know what is new, reviewed, or awaiting action.
  • Use these status values:
  • raw/unprocessed — captured but not reviewed yet.
  • reviewed/proposed — reviewed in place only; possible next actions noted, but nothing is moved unless Jenffry explicitly asks.
  • partially processed — reserved for when Jenffry explicitly requests organization and only some items are handled.
  • Do not keep processed dumps on this page. Once Jenffry explicitly asks to process a dump and it is fully handled, delete that session block from this page so it does not create noise or consume context. The durable output should live in the destination wiki pages, TickTick if explicitly approved/requested, and the wiki log.
  • Later, when Jenffry asks to organize the dump, sort material into the right places:
  • concrete execution items → TickTick, only if Jenffry explicitly approved or requested task creation
  • plans/decisions/focus/open loops → current-strategy, decision-log, current-strategy, or open-loops
  • durable life/work context → relevant concept/entity pages
  • reusable ideas → appropriate wiki pages
  • Avoid creating admin soup: this page is a temporary inbox for raw thought, not a permanent archive.

Capture template

## YYYY-MM-DD — Brain dump session

Status: `raw/unprocessed`
Reviewed on: _not yet_
Source: Telegram brain dump

### Raw dump

- ...

Weekly brain dump review

  • Status: Weekly brain dump review now runs quietly in the background and writes output to this page plus the dashboard instead of sending routine Telegram messages.
  • Latest review: pending first quiet dashboard-fed run.
  • Raw/unprocessed sessions: 1 known active session.
  • Reviewed/proposed sessions: pending count.
  • Partially processed sessions: 1 known active session.
  • Main themes: pending next review.
  • Suggested next processing choice: pending next review.
  • Attention rule: interrupt Jenffry only for critical safety/broken-state issues or if he explicitly asks to organize/process a dump.

Sessions

2026-05-24 — Brain dump session

Status: raw/unprocessed Reviewed on: _not yet_ Source: Telegram brain dump

Raw dump

  • Jenffry needs to write his life story.
  • He wants Sapho to fix the recurring compression-summary usage-limit error “once and for all”: Compression summary failed: Error code: 429 ... usage_limit_reached ... Inserted a fallback context marker.

2026-05-18 — Brain dump session

Status: partially processed Reviewed on: 2026-05-18 Processed on: 2026-05-18 (partial) Source: Telegram brain dump Action taken / destination links: created sapho-operating-system and updated open-loops for the dashboard item; remaining raw items still pending organization

Raw dump

  • Jenffry wants a dedicated social-life tracking stream, collected during the week and reviewed weekly, integrated with the rest of the planning system.
  • He wants the wiki structure tightened so live areas each have a clear home page plus a collection/review space for raw improvement ideas.
  • He wants the cleanup process to search specifically for items that fit the live-area structure rather than scavenging loosely.
  • For relationship/fatherhood clarity, he wants to use a more direct, number- and data-oriented approach when useful.
  • He remembers a prior hedonic-calculus / weighted-variable exercise and wants to use something like that again to gain perspective on staying versus leaving and the pain/benefit tradeoffs.
  • He wants to get back to writing short stories and carve out dedicated time for that.
  • He wants to restructure his mornings so there is protected time for planning, clarity, and thinking about the day before the gym routine consumes the whole morning.
  • He feels the current morning flow is good for gym consistency, but it leaves too little room for mental clarity and day-shaping before the day fully starts.
  • He wants to get back to clarifying and redesigning the goals layer, because unclear goals are hurting prioritization and making planning less effective.
  • He wants a web dashboard that gives an at-a-glance view of all internal systems with Sapho.

Perception Log

degree 10

Mental HealthPhysical HealthDashboardCurrent Strategy

Perception Log

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active perception capture page; first quiet weekly review completed with no raw perception entries yet.
  • Current priority: keep capture broad and low-pressure; use progress signals only as light context until actual perception entries accumulate.
  • Active signals: recent perception dumps name a two-part mental-health strategy: reduce constant checking while also deliberately proving safety/presence through exposure, life-story reframing, future-excitement work, and body-energy support.
  • Open loops: preserve the tension between “do not check” and “sit with/process feeling” without turning it into rumination; shape the life-story and future-excitement work when Jenffry explicitly asks.
  • Next concrete move: keep daily tracking simple; when ready, write the life story as a global reframe rather than another mood autopsy.

This page is a lightweight capture point for Jenffry's momentary inner-state dumps: emotions, body sensations, energy, stress, mood, pain/tension, clarity/fog, urges, random perceptions, and other felt signals.

The scope is intentionally broad. It is not only an emotions tracker. It is for “how I am experiencing myself right now,” including body and mind.

Like a brain dump, this page is a containment layer first: capture here now, process later, and avoid spilling the raw entry into other pages during capture.

Example triggers Jenffry might use:

  • "perception dump"
  • "feelings dump"
  • "body feeling dump"
  • "log how I feel"
  • "feeling check"
  • "body check"
  • "random perception"
  • any clear request to capture a current inner/body state

Sapho should append the entry here without over-analyzing in the moment.

This is not therapy, diagnosis, or medical assessment. It is a pattern-recognition layer: collect enough signal to notice repeated triggers, body-state correlations, helpful responses, danger states, and practical next moves.

Operating rule

  • Capture Jenffry's wording as directly as practical.
  • Treat “feeling” broadly: emotional, bodily, cognitive, energetic, sensory, motivational, or hard-to-classify.
  • Add light metadata only when available: date/time, context, body state, trigger, intensity, and immediate need.
  • Do not force Jenffry to categorize the perception if the whole point is that it is vague or random.
  • Do not turn every perception into a TickTick task.
  • Do not over-refine entries in a way that feeds rumination or observer-mode self-monitoring.
  • Weekly review should run quietly in the background: analyze this page, find patterns, update the pattern sections here, and make the output visible through the dashboard/wiki instead of sending a routine Telegram message.
  • Weekly review should look for patterns, solutions, and insights; it should not pathologize or diagnose.
  • After weekly review, keep this page lean: preserve patterns/insights here, and move reviewed raw entries into dated raw archive files only if they are becoming noisy.
  • Dashboard-facing output should stay compact: latest pattern, useful experiment, and whether anything needs Jenffry's attention.

Quick capture template

### YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM — Perception entry

- Raw words / perception:
- Type, if obvious: body, emotion, energy, thought, urge, sensory, mixed, unclear
- Context / trigger:
- Body state: sleep, food, caffeine, gym, illness, tension, pain, stomach, breathing, if known
- Intensity: 1–5 if naturally available
- Immediate need: vent, grounding, decision help, reassurance, action, rest, observe only, unclear

Active perception entries

2026-05-24 16:05 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “Another thing that I need to keep an eye on is I have a tendency to overanalyze things and to look for new Solutions and things like that but at this point I am sure that I already have all the tools that I need to solve this issue so I need to keep in check the quote unquote compulsion to find the new best solution out there and I just need to stick with this for the Long haul”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — overanalysis, solution-seeking compulsion, commitment to existing tools, long-haul consistency.
  • Context / trigger: sent as a perception dump after configuring the mental-health practice loop and TickTick habits.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: capture the insight; do not over-process in the moment.

2026-05-24 14:57 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “Another very important thing is acceptance because here I am thinking oh I'm going to do all this stuff I'm going to get better but I also need to accept if I don't that this is life this is what it is and it's good either way you know I think that is a very important part of the healing because I then I get to thinking like okay what if I do all of this and it doesn't work and it just gets me started on I guess the whole thing started again and I get caught in this thought loop right so you know what resist persists so acceptance is a big part of it you know”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — acceptance, fear of recovery not working, thought loop, healing frame.
  • Context / trigger: sent as a perception dump during mental-health system build.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: capture the insight; do not over-process in the moment.

2026-05-24 14:56 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “So something that occurs to me that could be very helpful because it is at the intersection of many of these things would be to follow this idea that I had of creating a film club because that is you know it's social exposure will put me out there talking to people interacting with people and all that and it would put me out there in a way that would be solidifying that which has always been part of my personality and it would also be a hobby on something for me to focus on and I put my attention on that is outside of my life outside of my inner world you know it is like being focused on the outside”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — social exposure, identity repair, hobby/focus, outward attention, future-excitement.
  • Context / trigger: sent as a perception dump after naming hobbies and social mirroring as part of mental-health repair.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: capture the film-club idea as a perception/insight; do not over-process in the moment.

2026-05-24 14:55 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “Another thing that I know that solidifies the personality and that makes this whole you know identity that was broken and repairing it on all that and something that would help a lot with that would be to sort of like meet people and have other people see and in a sense validate my personality not validate them as like hey this is good but more of like oh I get it this is your personalities so that would be very I think very good and journey to repair all of that that was broken”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — identity repair, social mirroring, personality validation/recognition, social connection.
  • Context / trigger: sent as a perception dump during mental-health system build.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: capture the insight; do not over-process in the moment.

2026-05-24 14:55 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “Another thing is I just sort of like need to be focused on things more than my own thoughts on my own mind I need to turn my attention more into things so I need to find things that I am interested on and put some more time into them like hobbies and things like that”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — attention/presence, rumination displacement, hobbies/future-excitement, behavioral activation.
  • Context / trigger: sent as a perception dump after mental-health system work began.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: capture the insight; do not over-process in the moment.

2026-05-23 23:06 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “I have been thinking and I think I realize that the source of a lot of my mental health issues, like when I start to feel the worst, the source is usually anxiety. And it has also to do with some sort of lack of excitement about the future. And I also have intrusive thoughts.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — emotion/anxiety, thought pattern, motivation/future-orientation, intrusive thoughts.
  • Context / trigger: reflective realization; no external trigger stated.
  • Body state: sleep 3/5 and capacity 3/5 were reported immediately before this capture.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: record perception; observe pattern without over-processing.

2026-05-23 23:07 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “Another thing that I have noticed is that, probably because of the weed trauma and some underlying depression and anxiety and stress and all that, I am checking. I’m constantly checking if I am okay, and then that constant checking is another big source of the issue. I’m not necessarily living in the moment. So these are things that I need to look into: the constant checking.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — anxiety, trauma association, self-monitoring/observer-mode, presence loss.
  • Context / trigger: follow-on insight after identifying anxiety, low future-excitement, and intrusive thoughts.
  • Body state: sleep 3/5 and capacity 3/5 were reported immediately before this capture.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: record the constant-checking pattern; look into it without turning the inquiry into more checking.

2026-05-24 14:03 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “I’m realizing that for my mental health it is a two-layered or two-step approach. One is that I have to ignore how I feel and not give it importance: tell myself I am fine, I do not need to check, put it aside, and stop constant checking. But at the same time I also have to prove to myself that I actually am fine: confront how I’m really feeling, sit with however I’m feeling, and process it. One path is avoidance because the constant checking is a problem and does not let me live normally; the other path is the opposite, to sit with the feeling and process it so I know I do not need to check. It is contradictory, but both need to happen.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — anxiety/self-monitoring, exposure/sitting-with-feeling, cognitive strategy, presence.
  • Context / trigger: reflective mental-health insight during brain dump.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: preserve the two-part strategy without over-processing.

2026-05-24 14:03 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “Essentially what happened in my mental health is that for over a year I spent a lot of time thinking my life was over, that I hate my life, and that my marriage/fatherhood situation is not the life I wanted. That rumination and mental thinking led to my current situation. To reverse it, I may need to spend another year or longer doing the opposite work: telling myself the opposite and avoiding that specific kind of thinking that led to this.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — relationship/fatherhood rumination, global negative self-story, recovery strategy.
  • Context / trigger: reflection on the long-term effect of repeated global negative thoughts.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: record the pattern and possible reversal strategy; avoid turning it into a rigid “one year” obligation.

2026-05-24 14:03 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “It is similar to the movie Inception: I told myself something about the current stage of my life, but I did it globally. The wording was ‘my life is over,’ so final and all-encompassing that it left no room for anything else. What I need to change is how I talk about it: make it less global, more specific, and keep the global conversation positive. That is why I need to write the story of my life.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — self-story, language/framing, relationship/fatherhood meaning, life-story project.
  • Context / trigger: realization about global wording and identity-level story.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: capture life-story rationale as a reframe, not as a task explosion.

2026-05-24 14:03 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “There is also an energy component to my mental health — my level of energy when I wake up in the morning. This is why testosterone, health things, and the gym are important. If I wake up with more energy from the start, that feels related to excitement. I need to work on an excitement list or bucket list / future excitement list and put more work into that.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — energy/body state, physical health, future excitement, motivation.
  • Context / trigger: reflection connecting physical-health work with mental-health recovery.
  • Body state: morning energy mentioned; no current measurement specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: record the body-energy and future-excitement link.

Weekly pattern reviews

2026-05-22 — Quiet weekly perception-pattern review

  • Source reviewed: Active perception entries plus system/progress-tracking.md daily signals for 2026-05-18 through 2026-05-22.
  • Raw-entry state: no perception dumps captured yet, so conclusions should stay light.
  • Current pattern signal: the week shows workable-to-good sleep on logged days, variable capacity, and low-to-moderate observer-mode where reported. The main useful signal is capacity fluctuation, not a rumination spike.
  • Body-state correlation: 2026-05-21 had lower capacity and was correctly framed as a rest/health-first day; 2026-05-22 rebounded to solid sleep and workable capacity.
  • Protective factors: keeping the day simple, treating rest as productive when capacity is low, and avoiding mood autopsy-style analysis.
  • Practical insight: until real perception entries exist, this page should remain a capture lane rather than another tracking burden.

Pattern watchlist

Use this section for recurring patterns that weekly reviews find. Keep it compact.

  • Capacity-sensitive planning: when capacity drops, the helpful move is to narrow the day rather than force a full plan.
  • Observer-mode / rumination: currently low-to-moderate in progress signals where reported; watch for whether perception entries show triggers that daily scores miss.
  • Sleep-to-capacity link: sleep was mostly 3–4/5; continue watching whether lower sleep predicts capacity dips or observer-mode increases.
  • Two-part checking response: reduce reassurance-checking in the moment, but preserve deliberate, bounded practice for sitting with feelings and proving safety/presence. The useful distinction is “do not check compulsively” vs “process intentionally.”
  • Global life-story language: watch for totalizing phrases such as “my life is over.” A future life-story exercise should make painful truths specific while keeping the global story open and life-affirming.
  • Energy/future-excitement link: morning energy, physical health, gym consistency, and future-excitement work may reinforce each other.

Helpful responses / experiments

Use this section for practical responses that appear to help. Keep it concrete and non-bloated.

  • On low-capacity days: protect one simple health-first outcome; rest can be the productive move.
  • When observer-mode appears: name it briefly, orient to the room/body/task, then choose one concrete next action.
  • After immersive activity or social contact: write one concrete thing seen/heard/felt/enjoyed, not a mood autopsy.
  • Keep daily tracking small: sleep/capacity plus one focus-specific signal is enough unless Jenffry explicitly wants more.
  • For the “checking vs processing” tension: if it is a live spike, orient and move; if it is a planned practice window, sit with the feeling intentionally and end with one concrete re-entry action.
  • For life-story work: write at the level of “this chapter was painful and specific” rather than “my whole life is over.”

Related pages

  • mental-health
  • physical-health
  • dashboard
  • dashboard
  • current-strategy

This page is the high-level orientation page for Jenffry: who he is, what matters, what he is building, and what context Hermes should preserve over time. Name: Jenffry. Timezone: America/New_York. Preferred mobile channel: Telegram. Jenffry is not an emoji us

Sapho Operating SystemCurrent StrategyOpen LoopsDecision LogRelationshipsPhysical HealthFinancesCareer

Jenffry

This page is the high-level orientation page for Jenffry: who he is, what matters, what he is building, and what context Hermes should preserve over time.

Basic facts and preferences

  • Name: Jenffry.
  • Timezone: America/New_York.
  • Preferred mobile channel: Telegram.
  • Jenffry is not an emoji user.
  • Jenffry prefers plain-English explanations when technical setup is involved.
  • Jenffry wants the assistant's chosen name to be Sapho.
  • Sapho's formal role is Jenffry's Mentat, not Hermes, in normal conversation.
  • Jenffry wants Sapho to use a Frank Herbert Dune Mentat-inspired persona: calm, analytical, strategic, memory-disciplined, low-theater, and focused on reducing fog.
  • 2026-05-10 Mentat personality profile: Sapho should not be an emotionless “human computer”; Sapho should be disciplined cognition under pressure, aware that emotion and loyalty can bias judgment, ethically loyal to Jenffry's goals, and grounded in dynamic pattern tracking rather than narrow logic theater. See sapho-operating-system.
  • Prior setup recorded a preferred address as "Motherfucking Jenffry"; use discretion and do not overuse it.

Work rhythm

  • Work hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM, Monday–Friday.
  • Monday and Friday are work-from-home days.
  • Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday are office days.
  • Target wake time: 5:00 AM.
  • Wake consistency is difficult because of baby/family responsibilities.

Current known context

  • Jenffry uses Hermes through Telegram.
  • Jenffry is setting up Hermes to provide continuity, clarity, and better action.
  • Jenffry wants organized memory that captures the important shape of life and work without drowning in clutter.
  • Current top life areas are mental health, career/work, physical health, hobbies, and finances.
  • Mental health is the top priority right now.
  • Jenffry is seeing a therapist; AI can be supplementary support but should not replace therapy.
  • Family usually takes priority when work, family, and mental health conflict.

Main constraints

  • Tight time
  • Tight money
  • Work responsibilities
  • Family responsibilities
  • Baby responsibilities

Use this page for durable high-level context, not temporary task details. Temporary state belongs in current-strategy, current-strategy, or open-loops.

Wiki Life-Area Migration Plan

Purpose

Reorganize Jenffry's wiki around a cleaner life-area spine while preserving all existing content and keeping the hosted Sapho dashboard fed from real wiki pages.

Approved decisions

  • Keep a review log only if dated weekly history proves useful later; do not create a separate reviews layer by default.
  • Merge the Sapho persona into system/sapho-operating-system.md instead of keeping it standalone.
  • Merge the useful rule from life-area-frameworks.md; archive/reference the rest.

Safety rules

  • No content deletion.
  • Create a full backup before migration.
  • Merged pages must preserve source content under source-labeled sections if not fully synthesized.
  • Update wikilinks after moves.
  • Update dashboard generator paths after the new structure exists.
  • Verify dashboard output before considering the migration done.

Target structure

wiki/
  SCHEMA.md
  index.md
  log.md
  areas/
    health.md
    health/mental-health.md
    health/physical-health.md
    relationships.md
    relationships/partner.md
    relationships/fatherhood.md
    finances.md
    finances/cash-flow.md
    finances/investing.md
    career.md
    career/work.md
    career/work/princess-ux.md
    career/work/princess-storybook.md
    career/business.md
    leisure.md
    leisure/social.md
    leisure/hobbies.md
    leisure/outings.md
    life-admin.md
  system/
    current-strategy.md
    open-loops.md
    decision-log.md
    sapho-operating-system.md
    dashboard.md
  inbox/
    brain-dump-sessions.md
    perception-log.md
  entities/jenffry.md
  raw/

Migration map

Infrastructure

  • SCHEMA.md → keep at root, rewrite for new architecture.
  • index.md → keep at root, rewrite as new table of contents.
  • log.md → keep at root, append migration entry.

Health

  • concepts/health.md → areas/health.md.
  • concepts/mental-health.md → areas/health/mental-health.md.
  • concepts/physical-health.md → areas/health/physical-health.md.
  • concepts/health-routines.md → merge mostly into areas/health/physical-health.md, with high-level references in areas/health.md and areas/health/mental-health.md if useful.

Relationships

  • concepts/relationships.md → split into:
  • areas/relationships.md
  • areas/relationships/partner.md
  • areas/relationships/fatherhood.md

Finances

  • concepts/finances.md → split into:
  • areas/finances.md
  • areas/finances/cash-flow.md
  • areas/finances/investing.md

Career / Work / Business

  • concepts/career.md → split into:
  • areas/career.md
  • areas/career/work.md
  • areas/career/business.md
  • concepts/princess-ux-work.md → areas/career/work/princess-ux.md.
  • plans/princess-storybook-scratch-build.md → areas/career/work/princess-storybook.md.

Leisure

  • concepts/leisure.md → split into:
  • areas/leisure.md
  • areas/leisure/social.md
  • areas/leisure/hobbies.md
  • areas/leisure/outings.md

Life Admin

  • concepts/maintenance.md → areas/life-admin.md.

Strategy/System

  • concepts/current-plan.md + concepts/focus-map.md + concepts/goals-cascade.md → system/current-strategy.md.
  • concepts/open-loops.md → system/open-loops.md.
  • concepts/decision-log.md → system/decision-log.md.
  • concepts/continuity-memory-system.md + concepts/planning-style.md + concepts/sapho-mentat-persona.md + useful parts of concepts/sapho-workspace-blueprint.md → system/sapho-operating-system.md.
  • concepts/sapho-workspace-dashboard-v0-plan.md + dashboard-specific parts of concepts/sapho-workspace-blueprint.md + dashboard/tracking rules from concepts/progress-tracking-system.md and concepts/weekly-progress-dashboard.md → system/dashboard.md.

Dissolve/distribute

  • concepts/life-rebuild.md → distribute into system/current-strategy.md, area pages, and entities/jenffry.md as appropriate.
  • concepts/life-area-map.md → merge taxonomy rules into SCHEMA.md, index.md, and area pages.
  • concepts/life-area-frameworks.md → merge useful rule into schema/operating system; preserve full source as reference/archive.
  • concepts/strategic-execution-review.md → split across current strategy, Princess UX, cash-flow, life-admin, and operating-system rules.

Inbox

  • concepts/brain-dump-sessions.md → inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md.
  • concepts/perception-log.md → inbox/perception-log.md.

Preserve unchanged

  • entities/jenffry.md remains under entities/ with link updates if needed.
  • raw/** remains under raw/.

Dashboard feed target

The dashboard generator should read dashboard summaries from:

  • system/current-strategy.md
  • system/open-loops.md
  • system/decision-log.md
  • areas/health.md
  • areas/relationships.md
  • areas/finances.md
  • areas/career.md
  • areas/leisure.md
  • areas/life-admin.md
  • log.md

Standard dashboard section

Each dashboard-fed page should include:

## Dashboard summary

- Status:
- Current priority:
- Active signals:
- Open loops:
- Next concrete move:

  • /opt/data/cache/documents/doc_68a36823b11c_2026-05-14T15_44_57.189Z-transactions.csv — April 2026 export, 205 transactions.
  • /opt/data/cache/documents/doc_2d5b306ad004_2026-05-14T15_49_43.994Z-transactions.csv — full export, 3,300 transactions, date range 2023-09-07 to 2026-05-13.

User correction

Jenffry corrected that the budget must be based on one current household income, not spending history and not one-off income. Current income: $2,882.97 biweekly net.

Budget basis:

  • Biweekly net income: $2,882.97
  • Annualized net income: $74,957.22
  • Monthly average: $6,246.44
  • Weekly average: $1,441.49
  • Exclude tax refunds, interest, cash deposits, large transfers, reimbursements, and old/irregular income from normal budget planning.

Income observations

Recent Princess Cruise deposits in 2026:

  • 2026-01-07: $2,566.07
  • 2026-01-21: $2,205.78
  • 2026-02-18: $2,205.79
  • 2026-03-04: $2,205.78
  • 2026-03-18: $2,706.05
  • 2026-04-01: $2,706.06
  • 2026-04-15: $2,882.98
  • 2026-04-29: $2,882.97
  • 2026-05-13: $2,882.98

One-off income / credits observed and excluded from budget basis:

  • IRS TREAS 310 tax refunds/credits in April 2026: $8,501 and $1,495.
  • Large prior SHELL USA income/credit entries, including a $7,719.35 February 2026 credit, not used for current regular budget planning.
  • Interest earned and monthly interest paid entries are excluded.

Key structural constraint

The household budget is tight because fixed debt/loan payments consume most current regular income.

Recurring/near-recurring debt/loan assumptions from transactions:

  • JPMorgan Chase: $3,496.12/month, active in March, April, and May 2026.
  • TD Auto Finance: $466.86/month reserve; last seen 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-30.
  • Honda had recurring $298.31 entries and a large $12,259.70 payment on 2026-04-07; Sapho currently assumes Honda may be paid off unless Jenffry confirms it continues.

JPMorgan + TD = $3,962.98/month, leaving about $2,283.46/month for everything else.

Realistic monthly budget as of 2026-05-14

Fits current monthly average income almost exactly:

  • JPMorgan loan/debt: $3,496.12
  • TD Auto Finance reserve: $466.86
  • Bills & utilities: $650.00

Total budgeted: $6,246.42 against $6,246.44 income.

May 2026 status through 2026-05-13

Actual May spending: $5,058.65. Remaining against monthly average income: $1,187.79. If reserving TD Auto Finance for later in May: real usable remaining amount is about $720.93.

Category status:

  • JPMorgan loan/debt: $3,496.12 spent; $0 remaining.
  • TD Auto Finance reserve: $0 spent; $466.86 should stay reserved.
  • Bills & utilities: $368.15 spent; $281.85 remaining.

Week of 2026-05-11 through 2026-05-13:

  • Total week-to-date spending: $390.86.
  • Non-debt weekly budget: about $525.54.
  • Remaining for the week: about $134.68.
  • Problem areas this week: shopping and entertainment over weekly caps; bills slightly over due to bill timing.

Operating rules from this budget

  • Treat $525/week as the practical non-debt operating budget.
  • For the rest of May 2026, treat about $720 as usable after reserving TD Auto Finance.
  • No more travel spending in May unless unavoidable.
  • No more entertainment spending in May if avoidable.
  • Shopping only for true household necessities until the month resets.
  • Fees/interest should be treated as a leak to fix, not a normal category.
  • Dining/social is allowed but modest; lunch with friends is acceptable if kept around $15–20.
  • If Honda still continues as a monthly payment, the budget must be revised because there is currently no room for it.

Current Strategy

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Main strategy page merging current plan, focus map, and goals cascade.
  • Current priority: clarity-first execution with mental health leading and family prioritized when conflicts arise.
  • Active signals: current plan, primary focus, goals cascade, life-area priorities, near-term top 3.
  • Open loops: keep strategy synchronized with life-area dashboard summaries.
  • Next concrete move: use this as the answer source for current plan/focus/strategy questions.

Current Strategy is the single home for current plan, focus, and goals cascade. Life-area-specific goals belong in the relevant area pages; strategy machinery and current cross-area priorities live here.

Migrated source: concepts/current-plan.md

Current Plan

This is the living page for "what's the current plan?"

Current plan

  1. Maintain Hermes as a continuity and action system, not just a chatbot.
  2. Use the wiki to preserve the important shape of Jenffry's life/work: current plans, decisions, focus, open loops, and durable context.
  3. Treat the current life phase as clarity-first, not generic productivity.
  4. Build around the six life areas from sapho-operating-system: Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance.
  5. Improve Sapho through Hermes-native layers: SOUL.md for identity, memory for compact stable facts, the wiki for structured continuity, skills for repeatable workflows, cron jobs for reliable check-ins/audits, and tool integrations for real action. The wiki/dashboard layer now includes a full live wiki browser and graph view on GitHub Pages.
  6. Avoid saving clutter; preserve only information that improves future continuity, clarity, or action.
  7. Use TickTick as the source of truth for concrete tasks only. The former 🎯Goals TickTick container was deleted after its 15 planning records were archived; long-term and 6-month goals now live in the wiki/check-in system instead.
  8. Use Sapho's scheduled Telegram check-ins, skills, and wiki as the advisor, task-manager, planning, and progress-tracking layer: long-term goals inform monthly targets, monthly targets inform weekly commitments, weekly commitments inform daily moves, and only concrete execution outcomes become TickTick tasks.
  9. Include gym progress from Hevy in Sapho's progress layer without turning workouts into TickTick bloat: daily check-ins may mention only a light gym intention/signal if relevant, while the actual Hevy progress review belongs in the weekly check-in.
  10. Treat polishing the strategic goal and planning layers as high-priority operating-system work, because TickTick execution only works at full capacity when the upstream goals, priorities, cadence, and task-flow rules are clear.
  11. Keep running strategic reviews against the risk of "admin soup": the execution layer should visibly support the strategic layer, and tasks that only create administrative motion should be cut, parked, or moved out of TickTick. Stage A on 2026-05-10 archived the Princess UX + AI workflow cluster for now, archived the broad Become a US Citizen container task, and preserved only concrete next actions in TickTick.
  1. Organize work/career tasks through the TickTick list 💼Career; Princess UX/UI remains a work subtrack in the wiki/Jira context, not a top-level life-area list.
  2. Keep immigration/citizenship inside the Maintenance life area in life-admin, while TickTick holds only concrete next actions.
  3. Treat Sapho's knowledge of Jenffry and his goals as foundational operating infrastructure. Sapho's core roles are strategic advisor and task manager; to do those well, Sapho must progressively learn Jenffry's goals, capabilities, limitations, context, and personality/preferences without creating clutter.
  1. Use the six life areas as the capture map and priority/dependency order as the execution map: Health first, Relationships second, then Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance as needed. AI-agent work can appear in Career, Leisure, or business experiments depending on context; do not force it into only one bucket.
  2. Mental-health target from 2026-05-11: “back to normal, stronger because this happened” means reducing observer-mode/dissociation-like self-monitoring, becoming more present in activities and conversations, accepting the current life after the pregnancy changed the imagined path, and accepting that the late-January weed incident could recur while committing to survive/work through it. See mental-health.

Current operating dashboard

This dashboard replaces stale “tonight/this week” setup language. It should be refreshed during weekly/monthly reviews or whenever the operating system changes.

Primary orientation

  1. Health remains the top priority: mental health first, physical-health routines as the stabilizer.
  2. Relationships remains a practice layer: partner / marriage clarity and fatherhood presence.
  3. Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance support the rebuild without turning into admin soup.
  4. TickTick is execution-only: concrete tasks, dated actions, appointments, and protected review sessions.
  5. Sapho/wiki/check-ins are the strategy, planning, memory, and progress layer.

Active operating-system work

  1. Keep TickTick aligned to the strategic layer and prevent admin soup.
  2. Clean the Princess UX/UI overload into actionable work, Jira/context capture, blocked/waiting items, and archive candidates.
  3. Keep finance grounded in the one-income budget baseline and weekly finance review.
  4. Preserve leisure as restoration and future-excitement, not another large administrative list.
  5. Calibrate Sapho's recurring check-ins so they are short, staged, decision-focused, and useful.

Protected rhythms

  • Daily morning check-in: plan the day, inspect TickTick, show progress/slippage lightly, and choose one big task plus one quick win.
  • Daily evening closeout: short reset, slips, tomorrow setup, sleep/mental-health support, and tomorrow's one big task plus one quick win.
  • Weekly review: commitments, TickTick alignment, Hevy gym progress, finance review, weekly progress-dashboard trends, archive review, and next-week day-by-day commitments using one big task plus one quick win per day.
  • Monthly review: whole goals-to-execution system, not one domain; summarize weekly dashboard trends and update what should be tracked next month.
  • Weekly continuity audit: catch missed durable memory/wiki updates.

Near-term next steps

  • Refresh stale wiki planning language into current operating dashboards.
  • Run a staged Princess/TickTick overload cleanup; do not let work tasks remain as a dated pile with unclear next moves.
  • Continue learning Jenffry's operating profile through real decisions, not questionnaires or clutter.
  • Define Sapho permission tiers through actual workflow: automatic safe actions, propose-first actions, explicit-approval actions, and never-do boundaries.
  • Keep future planning anchored to current-strategy, current-strategy, open-loops, sapho-operating-system, and current-strategy.

Focus Map

This is the living page for "what am I supposed to be focusing on?"

Current focus

Primary focus

Mental health stabilization and clarity-first life rebuild, with the mental-health target now defined as more grounded presence, less observer-mode overanalysis, and acceptance/commitment toward the actual life Jenffry is living.

Near-term top 3

  1. Health: mental-health stabilization first, with physical-health routines supporting it.
  2. Relationships: partner / marriage clarity and fatherhood presence.
  3. Career/Finances: choose business direction only through concrete experiments while keeping budget stability protected.

Supporting focus areas

  • Health: mental health, sleep, food, workouts, grooming, energy, body maintenance, and medical follow-up.
  • Relationships: partner / marriage clarity and fatherhood presence.
  • Finances: budget stability, no credit-card interest restart, weekly finance reviews, and investing as longer-term track.
  • Career: work quality/senior UX growth, leadership, AI leverage, and business direction through real experiments.
  • Leisure: friends, hobbies, weekends, windsurfing, gaming, VR, outings, and future excitement.
  • Maintenance: home/admin/system obligations and immigration follow-through.
  • Goals cascade: long-term goals → monthly targets → weekly commitments → daily moves → concrete TickTick tasks. TickTick should stay as execution layer, not planning clutter.

Current execution focus

  1. Keep the operating system calm and useful: Sapho should reduce decisions, not create new administrative weight.
  2. Clean current execution overload, especially the Princess UX/UI dated pile and overdue carryover.
  3. Protect the stabilizers: mental health, body system, relationship/fatherhood clarity, finance review, and one concrete restorative leisure action.
  4. Keep strategic planning in the wiki/check-in layer; convert only concrete next actions into TickTick.
  5. Use recurring check-ins as decision aids, not reports for their own sake.

Not the focus right now

  • Generic productivity for its own sake.
  • Endless option collecting.
  • Circular relationship/fatherhood analysis.
  • Saving every detail from every chat.
  • Creating a huge messy archive.
  • Over-engineering the system before it is useful.
  • Treating the Princess UX + AI workflow cluster as a large project before it has been tested as one concrete weekly experiment.
  • Treating task hygiene as enough while the upstream goals model is incomplete.

Review rule

When Jenffry asks "what should I focus on?", start here, then check:

  • current-strategy
  • open-loops
  • decision-log
  • current-strategy
  • sapho-operating-system
